166994.explanation: Residents must be involved in decisions that affect them. Participation like consultation, thinking along, citizen panels, and surveys is important. Because complex questions often cannot be answered with only yes/no, we support an advisory referendum, not binding.

166996.explanation: Shops may be open on Sundays for shopkeepers and customers who want this. It is naturally not mandatory. This makes it much easier for singles who work during the week to do important shopping.

166998.explanation: Refugees and other newcomers must be able to participate quickly. We want humane help, where we work together with residents to ensure people quickly feel at home in our municipality. We have done this well together with the reception of Ukrainians.

167000.explanation: We celebrate the best New Year's party together. We uphold the national fireworks ban. We prefer to offer a laser show because fireworks burden the environment and cause animal suffering. Activities for children and youth on New Year’s Eve receive financial support.

167001.explanation: A field with solar panels can supply many households with clean, affordable energy. If farmers want, they should be able to use their land for this. Solar panels can be well combined with increased biodiversity, for example by allowing flowers and other low greenery to grow in the field.

167002.explanation: Every child should learn to swim. School swimming is a solution in neighborhoods where this really doesn’t work, but not throughout the whole municipality. In De Bilt we already financially support parents who cannot pay for swimming lessons, so every child can obtain a swimming diploma.

167003.explanation: Sports give a lot of energy and satisfaction. They keep you healthy and often the sports club is also a meeting place. When a sports club fails, the whole village suffers. We believe the municipality should (in time) step in if needed to prevent clubs from failing.

167004.explanation: We choose to have more enforcement officers on the streets. They can intervene immediately when something goes wrong. We allocate extra resources to have enough public order officers to supervise and counteract nuisance, working together with youth workers where necessary.

167005.explanation: In our opinion, this need is already sufficiently met in our municipality, and active opposition is, in our view, not necessary. If new applications come from initiators, we will assess them properly according to the rules.

167006.explanation: We want to preserve and protect the greenery around our residential area as best as possible. We strengthen the green buffer between us and the city and cherish our beautiful landscape and views. We build within the built-up area, close to amenities. We do not build on meadows or natural areas.

167008.explanation: The housing market is stuck. Many people wait up to 12 years for a social rental home. We believe this must improve. In new projects, we want at least 40% of the homes to be social rental for residents of our municipality. Additionally, we want 20% mid-rent and affordable owner-occupied homes.

167009.explanation: We need to build homes for our youth and elderly. Sometimes that can be somewhat taller, provided it fits the surroundings, up to approximately 7 residential floors. High apartment buildings like those in the city do not fit well in our villages.

167010.explanation: Housing is a fundamental right, not a profit model. We support the owner-occupancy obligation so that our houses are not traded for profit. For new homes up to about 600,000 euros, an owner-occupancy obligation and an anti-speculation clause apply.

167011.explanation: Holiday homes are not a permanent solution to the housing shortage. But as long as the need is high, some people can stay in them. It is important that they can use the holiday home as a fixed (mailing) address as long as necessary.

167013.explanation: Sometimes it makes sense that someone temporarily does not apply for jobs. It is important to consider the situation of the informal caregiver and the applicable rules. This must be properly agreed upon and monitored.

167014.explanation: People need certainty and trust from the municipality. Extra rules and conditions create uncertainty and new problems. That is why we choose a different approach. We build a system for automatic granting of benefits for residents with low incomes.

167015.explanation: We consider electric driving important. We designate more parking spaces as charging points. We encourage the use of electric (shared) cars by placing enough public charging stations in every village and neighborhood.

167016.explanation: If necessary for comfortable living, we can work with parking permits. Where space is limited, we prioritize affordable housing over additional parking spots. In logical places, such as where public transport is good, we reduce the number of parking spaces.

167017.explanation: The figures clearly show: with a lower maximum speed, traffic in our municipality becomes much safer. For cycling children and elderly people with mobility issues, this makes a world of difference. Furthermore, the streets are quieter and the air cleaner.

167018.explanation: Everyone must be treated equally. People may choose their own care provider, but that may not lead to discrimination. We do not approve of refusing a caregiver, for example because of a headscarf. We do understand that it helps if caregiver and client understand each other well.

167019.explanation: Choosing the cheapest home care does not help people. We choose quality and certainty. Therefore, we set strict requirements for home care organizations and allow fewer of them. This ensures that the care matches what people need.

167020.explanation: There will be one counter in neighborhoods and villages. People can go there with questions about money and support. The counter also helps to raise awareness of schemes such as Stichting Leergeld, the U-pass, and the Sports & Culture Fund. This way, more people know their rights and where to get help.

167022.explanation: We currently cooperate with five other municipalities in ICT. By expanding this cooperation, our organization becomes stronger and we save costs. Cooperation in other areas can also help to work smarter and save money.

167023.explanation: Diversity is not a side issue for us. The municipality wants to be an organization where everyone is treated equally. We incorporate inclusivity and equality in everything we do. Diversity helps to connect with all people in our municipality, in a society where everyone counts.

167024.explanation: Accountability is already very extensive - digitally and via the newspaper - and we try to improve it. We take into account people who have difficulty reading and limited digital skills. This means ensuring our communication is accessible to everyone.

167025.explanation: Some shops remain empty for a very long time. That is a waste of scarce space. Therefore, we ensure that shops and businesses are not vacant but used. We do this through active vacancy policies, focusing on the biggest problem, the vacancy in De Kwinkelier.

167026.explanation: We do not want to pay off debts as quickly as possible if it comes at the expense of our residents. We continue to invest in things that matter, such as affordable housing, schools, the library, the Arts House, places for youth, and nature. Our municipal debt is well below the norm.

167027.explanation: Every municipality must take in its share of refugees, so the municipality of De Bilt cooperates in this as well. We want to consider the best location and appropriate reception.

167028.explanation: Eating vegetarian significantly reduces greenhouse gas emissions, water use, land use, and waterway pollution. We encourage vegetarian eating, but we do not want to oblige our colleagues in the municipal council.

167029.explanation: Our municipality needs solar and wind energy to become climate neutral. We are looking for a location for wind energy in our municipality. We do this as much as possible together with residents. Neighbors who are affected are compensated.

167030.explanation: We want to better protect animals and their habitats. Therefore, we allocate funds for a full-time official for animal welfare and biodiversity. Events involving animals must also be good for the animals. Organizers must demonstrate in permit applications how they ensure this.

167031.explanation: Together with the province, we are starting a public heating company. This gives residents more control over their own heating supply. For each neighborhood and village, we will create a sustainability plan for greenery, water, and energy. Everyone can participate, but this is never mandatory.

167032.explanation: We tackle water nuisance and drought by letting rainwater flow into the ground instead of the sewer. We do this, for example, with permeable paving and retention basins. In construction projects, climate-friendly, clean, and low-emission building is given significant weight.

167033.explanation: The old town hall Jagtlust and a large part of the site are already municipal monuments. We want to investigate the possibility of a new town hall – of which the manor Jagtlust will be part. Of course, we will handle our heritage respectfully.

167034.explanation: A library in the neighborhood is more than just a collection of books. It is a place to meet, to work on language assistance, or for associations to gather. Library Points strengthen a village or neighborhood. Therefore, we want them to reopen.

167036.explanation: Agreed! We want a new Bilts stage and cinema for everyone, but especially with a focus on youth. Julianalaan 1 in Bilthoven is a good location. There should be more activities for young people in the municipality.

167038.explanation: Dimming street lighting at night is important for our nature. It protects ecosystems from disruption caused by light pollution. This helps insects, bats, and migratory birds. Brighter light at night usually does not mean more safety for women; it creates a dark zone around.

167039.explanation: In 2023 we agreed not to establish intensive forensic care in our municipality. We already have relatively many institutions for psychiatric care here. Forensic care is needed, however. Therefore, together with other municipalities in the region, we are looking for a suitable location.

167040.explanation: Violence and unwanted behavior towards women and girls must be addressed. This starts with raising children. This applies everywhere: at home, at school, at work, and in the municipal council. The safety and freedom of women and girls are our highest priority.

167041.explanation: The police station is currently located in the De Bilt town hall. Due to staff shortages and high costs, it is now only a support point. When building the new town hall, the municipality can investigate whether there will again be space for a police station there.

167042.explanation: The housing shortage, especially among young people, is enormous. They often still live at home out of necessity. Older people also cannot find suitable housing. Therefore, we will build what fits, not as little as possible. Housing near the station with a maximum of approximately 7 floors, close to shops and public transport, is a very good idea.

167044.explanation: Here lies the most vulnerable piece of nature network in the entire Netherlands. The closure of the car scrapyard is being used to deploy this area to strengthen nature and cultural history. The nature network there is already under strong pressure from housing on the adjacent Hessing site.

167045.explanation: We help young people with large debts get their lives back on track. The municipality takes over their debts if necessary and guides them towards work and/or school. Living with debts and poverty makes people-especially young people-vulnerable and insecure.

167046.explanation: We prefer searching a bit longer for a parking spot than years searching for a house. Paid parking or a permit system is not taboo for us if we have to choose between housing and parking spaces. Whether paid parking is needed in the center is uncertain. If so, the first hours should be free.

167047.explanation: If necessary for comfortable living, we can choose paid parking or parking permits. In logical places, we opt for fewer parking spaces, for example where public transport accessibility is good. Where space is limited, we make conscious choices.

167048.explanation: For us, the safety and comfort of pedestrians and cyclists take precedence over car accessibility. We have embraced “STOMP” as the basis for traffic policy (STOMP stands for Walking, Cycling, Public Transport, Mobility as a Service, Private Car).

167050.explanation: We do everything possible to support residents in poverty timely and properly. We use the legal options to the fullest and actively explore the limits of what is possible within national regulations, so that no one falls through the cracks.

167051.explanation: We want mixed housing types, in all shapes and sizes. The housing shortage is very large. We want to devote time and resources to all kinds of housing, especially for youth and elderly.

167052.explanation: We support associations, village halls, and community centers. De Vierstee, WVT, and H.F. Witte are great examples of community centers that connect young and old. We also want a new stage for young and old at Julianalaan 1.

167054.explanation: The waiting lists and problems in youth care are significant. Our young people come first. Costs are important, but not decisive. We must primarily ensure certainty and help that matches the concerns of our youth. We want young people to receive better appropriate care.

167055.explanation: Young people are under pressure from all sides, from unaffordable housing to addictive social media algorithms. Therefore, the municipality advocates through the VNG for free mental health care. With youth work and free mental health care, we prevent young people from (further) getting into problems.

167056.explanation: We are concerned about the increasing smartphone use among children and the effect of social media. Also, the social pressure parents feel to give their child a phone. We want to develop policies to promote less smartphone use among children under 15 years old.

166994.explanation: D66 wants residents to be able to use a corrective binding referendum. This means that residents can reverse decisions afterward. We prefer to use a citizens' assembly, where a randomly selected group of residents advises politics or helps decide on a subject.

166996.explanation: D66 wants shopkeepers to be able to determine their own opening hours.

166998.explanation: D66 wants newcomers to start working quickly, which requires good contact with businesses and entrepreneurs. D66 wants language lessons from day one and to ensure connection with the community. We want to connect volunteers with newcomers so they can engage together in society.

167000.explanation: D66 supports residents at the neighborhood level who want to celebrate New Year's Eve together. Through municipal budgets, residents can organize a suitable activity themselves, together with and/or for their neighborhood.

167001.explanation: D66 wants to realize two larger solar fields in De Bilt, including on agricultural land. We prefer locations along the highway. This way, we can better protect our nature.

167002.explanation: Learning to swim is important in our country. Swimming lessons are expensive. If you cannot afford it yourself, the municipality ensures you can still attend swimming lessons (for example via the U-pas or the Youth Fund for Sports & Culture). Teaching children to swim is not a school task; the curriculum is already full.

167003.explanation: Sports and culture are important for health. D66 wants everyone to be able to play sports, make music, or dance, etc. If you cannot afford it yourself, the municipality can help. D66 wants the municipality to assist associations in finding additional income.

167004.explanation: The environment where you live must be safe. Neighborhood coaches and youth work can help. D66 wants to be able to enforce rules when boundaries are crossed. We want to tackle the root causes of insecurity. Good cooperation with the offenders' environment can help.

167005.explanation: D66 chooses realistic and humane drug policy. Coffeeshops where legal drugs are sold are part of this. D66 assesses each location to determine if a coffeeshop is appropriate.

167006.explanation: The housing shortage is large; for young and old there are no affordable (single-level) homes. D66 De Bilt continues to build new homes, for example near Weltevreden street and in Maartensdijk-South. If necessary, this also requires housing construction at the edges of the existing village boundaries.

167008.explanation: These percentages do not fit with creating truly mixed neighborhoods. D66 De Bilt wants 30% of all new homes to be social rental housing and 20% mid-priced rental. This aligns with national guidelines and ensures mixed residential areas for every budget.

167009.explanation: The housing shortage is significant. D66 wants people to find a home in our municipality. Sometimes apartments fit with existing buildings and surroundings, such as at H.F. Witte or in the Spoorzone. D66 finds it important that houses are of good quality, well insulated, and attractive to young and old.

167010.explanation: Many homes were bought by investors. Cheap owner-occupied homes were bought out from under the noses of first-time buyers to rent out at high prices. That must no longer be allowed due to the Affordable Rent Act and De Bilt's buyout protection. D66 wants to continue this, otherwise, the De Bilt housing market becomes unreachable for first-time buyers.

167011.explanation: National policy is under development for this. D66 finds it important to adhere to laws and regulations. In principle, D66 wants holiday homes to also be allowed as a residential address. Under what conditions this can happen should be investigated.

167013.explanation: It is nice if a known person can support you when needed. Sometimes it is not possible to work alongside a caregiving role, but usually it is. D66 believes that in such cases, the circumstances of those involved must be carefully considered. Therefore, we choose tailored solutions.

167014.explanation: A full basic income is financially unfeasible. A reform of social security is necessary. D66 wants a system where freedom, justice, and solidarity are in balance. An unconditional basic income-a benefit for everyone-does not provide this balance.

167015.explanation: D66 wants as many people as possible to drive electric cars. Public charging stations are needed. Therefore, more charging stations must be installed. D66 also wants it to be easier for people to choose an electric shared car.

167016.explanation: D66 chooses the parking disc along with designating a blue zone. A combination with parking permits can be a solution with sufficient support. D66 finds it important that there is an alternative to the car. Good bicycle facilities and accessible, affordable public transport, for example.

167017.explanation: D66 wants as many roads in built-up areas as possible to be designated as 30 km/h zones. In some cases this seems unnecessary or impossible, for example Soestdijkseweg Zuid.

167018.explanation: Every healthcare provider must be able to empathize with every patient. They are trained and retrained for this. In addition, we find it important that every patient receives appropriate care. This can be in a hospital but increasingly at home in their own familiar environment.

167019.explanation: If you need home care, it is quite impactful. The municipality must provide appropriate care. Affordability is important, but this must not come at the expense of good care. D66 finds it important to use innovative ideas that make care better and cheaper.

167020.explanation: D66 wants a neighborhood hub or room in every district. People can meet there. People can work together to keep the neighborhood safe. Residents can help each other with digital and government matters. D66 wants to start this in neighborhoods where people need it most.

167022.explanation: D66 wants cooperation wherever possible. This often happens regionally with other municipalities. This makes it cheaper and better in quality.

167023.explanation: D66 wants municipal employees with diverse ideas, backgrounds, and insights. This way we closely match the composition of our municipality.

167024.explanation: D66 finds it important that residents understand how their tax money is spent. Communication in clear language is important for this. This can be done via the municipality's website, social media, and the newspaper. We find this more promising than spending extra money on an online data portal.

167025.explanation: D66 wants our shopping areas to remain lively. The municipality can invest in terraces, accessibility, greenery, and street furniture.

167026.explanation: Paying off debts is important. We do not want to do this by raising municipal taxes such as property tax. We only want these to rise with inflation. We do want to do this by building homes near schools, which will generate income. We also want to invest in projects with demonstrably positive returns.

167027.explanation: D66 prefers large-scale reception locations. Research and experience in other municipalities show these put less pressure on local facilities in residential areas such as the GP, pharmacy, and education.

167028.explanation: D66 finds it important that people can choose what to eat themselves. D66 wants a limited offer of high-quality, organic products with much attention to animal welfare. We mainly choose local entrepreneurs. This way we offer sales opportunities to nature-inclusive farmers.

167029.explanation: D66 wants wind energy to be generated in De Bilt as well, to become less dependent on energy from abroad and to contribute to the climate. We choose one wind turbine. Additionally, we have made it easier to place a small wind turbine on your own land.

167030.explanation: This has too little impact. D66 sees more opportunities in increasing knowledge about animal welfare and biodiversity among all municipal employees, for example through e-learning. This way, they can automatically take biodiversity and animal welfare into account.

167031.explanation: D66 wants to move away from natural gas as much as possible. We support the ambition of natural gas-free neighborhoods. We do not choose forced participation. We prefer to work well with residents of the relevant neighborhood from the start. For this, we make clear agreements.

167032.explanation: D66 wants new residential areas to be designed climate-resilient to prevent nuisance for residents. Better to invest upfront than to repair later at higher costs.

167033.explanation: Jagtlust is an iconic building in De Bilt. We definitely want to preserve it. D66 sees no added value in designating the land behind the town hall as a municipal monument. This would limit opportunities for small-scale housing.

167034.explanation: D66 wants neighborhood hubs or rooms in every district where people can meet. Residents can help each other with digital and government matters. This can be combined with a library function. Preferably, we see these places run by social entrepreneurs.

167036.explanation: D66 is open to supporting a film house in Bilthoven if there is sufficient support among residents. We then want a social entrepreneur who wants to facilitate this.

167038.explanation: D66 believes it is important that people feel safe. Dangerous locations should be well lit. D66 recognizes the value of a dark night for people and nature. D66 wants a careful consideration to always be made when choosing to add extra street lighting.

167039.explanation: D66 considers appropriate care essential when needed. D66 believes De Bilt is currently making a good contribution. If psychiatric care is necessary for the region, D66 is open to its expansion. We want clear frameworks from the outset and will consult with the community.

167040.explanation: D66 wants to improve the safety of women as quickly as possible, both on the streets and behind closed doors. We want to involve experts with lived experience, such as the Soroptimists, in drafting and implementing policy.

167041.explanation: D66 does not see direct added value in opening a police station in De Bilt. D66 finds visible enforcement on the streets important. We can deploy neighborhood hubs or rooms in every district where help can be offered, for example with filing reports.

167042.explanation: D66 considers building 750 homes in the Spoorzone a good number. The housing shortage is large, and the area can accommodate this number well. We especially want to build affordable homes for young people and seniors. This decision has already been made. Reversing decisions does not suit a reliable government.

167044.explanation: D66 wants to build additional homes, including on the site of this former car scrapyard. The housing shortage is significant, and building homes will give this location a nice, new purpose.

167045.explanation: D66 wants to focus on debt prevention with low-threshold support. For problematic debts, existing arrangements can already be used, including debt restructuring (such as taking over debts).

167046.explanation: D66 sees the parking disc as the designated way to reduce parking pressure. D66 is open to paid parking and parking permits as long as there is broad support for this in a neighborhood.

167047.explanation: D66 opts for the parking disc combined with designating a blue zone. Combining this with parking permits for residents can be a solution. D66 finds it important that there is a full alternative to the car. Bicycle facilities and accessible, affordable public transport can reduce car ownership.

167048.explanation: D66 wants public space to be designed first from the perspective of pedestrians and cyclists, and only afterwards from the perspective of motorists.

167050.explanation: D66 wants children to grow up free from poverty. We choose measures that reduce people’s fixed costs, such as insulating homes. If people are in financial distress, the municipality should be a safe place to support them.

167051.explanation: D66 wants more housing options for seniors between living independently and nursing homes.

167052.explanation: D66 wants a neighborhood hub or community room in every district. People can meet there, keep the neighborhood safe together, and help each other with digital and government-related matters. D66 wants to start this in districts where it is most needed.

167054.explanation: D66 wants to strongly invest in youth care prevention to reduce suffering and costs. The municipality of De Bilt spends much more on youth care than comparable municipalities. We want to learn from the experiences of others so our costs decrease.

167055.explanation: D66 wants to step up youth care prevention to reduce hardship and costs. Young people who need help should be able to receive it. We expect a personal contribution from those who can afford to pay.

167056.explanation: D66 recognizes the downsides of social media and wants people to learn to use it more wisely. An awareness campaign can contribute to this.

166994.explanation: Decisions that have a large impact on the daily lives of residents require direct involvement. A referendum can give residents a clear voice in major choices. This strengthens ownership, trust, and decision-making by and for residents.

166996.explanation: Entrepreneurs should be able to decide themselves when to open. Flexible shop opening hours allow room for entrepreneurship and align with residents’ wishes. The municipality should offer freedom, not impose bans.

166998.explanation: Integration mainly requires participation: working, learning, and social contact in the neighborhood, not increasing spending. Extra expenditures without clear results do not help. A large-scale asylum seekers center at Berg en Bosch is unsuitable and pressures livability and safety.

167000.explanation: A central fireworks display ensures a safe, festive New Year’s Eve for everyone. It limits nuisance and risks in residential areas and brings residents together. This way, we combine conviviality with safety and livability.

167001.explanation: Agricultural land is meant for food production and landscape, not large-scale solar fields. Priority should first be on roofs, parking lots, and existing buildings. This keeps our farmland open, green, and economically usable for farmers.

167002.explanation: School swimming increases children’s safety and prevents unequal opportunities. Not every family can afford swimming lessons. Supporting school swimming is an investment in safety, health, and equal starting chances for all children in De Bilt.

167003.explanation: Sports clubs are important, but financial support is not automatic. The municipality assesses each situation to determine if support is necessary and responsible. Self-reliance and club initiatives remain the starting point.

167004.explanation: Visible enforcement increases safety and the feeling of safety. More community officers (BOAs) ensure faster action against nuisance and crime and strengthen cooperation with the police. This keeps De Bilt safe and livable for everyone.

167005.explanation: New coffee shops increase pressure on safety and livability. The municipality must prevent nuisance and crime and protect vulnerable neighborhoods. Enforcement and clear limits provide calm, safety, and a pleasant living environment.

167006.explanation: Building within the red contour protects the green and village character of De Bilt. Choose infill, reuse, and small-scale solutions. This preserves open landscape, prevents urbanization, and strengthens livability in our centers.

167008.explanation: A fixed 40% social rental quota hinders housing flow and makes building harder. De Bilt is already well above the national average. Opt for tailored solutions: more affordable home ownership and homes for starters and seniors, so the housing market really moves.

167009.explanation: High residential towers do not fit the village and green character of De Bilt. We do not build above the trees. Preference goes to small-scale homes with a garden, fitting the human scale and livability of our centers.

167010.explanation: A self-occupancy obligation prevents investors’ purchases, keeps homes available for starters and families, and contributes to fair chances in the De Bilt housing market.

167011.explanation: At parks where mostly permanent residence already exists, such as Floris V, legalization can help solve the housing crisis. This requires tailored approaches: not every park is suitable. Safety, quality, and livability are paramount.

167013.explanation: Informal caregiving (mantelzorg) is valuable, but full exemption from job application requirements is not always appropriate. Tailored approaches are needed: space for caregiving where possible, activation towards work where feasible.

167014.explanation: A basic income removes the incentive to participate and is financially irresponsible. The focus should be on activation, tailored support, and employment assistance. This helps people sustainably and keeps municipal finances healthy.

167015.explanation: Electric driving is growing, but public space is limited. Charging spots are necessary without causing extra parking pressure. The municipality must assess each neighborhood's needs, grid capacity, and alternatives like charging on private property.

167016.explanation: There is no structural parking problem in De Bilt. Parking permits or paid parking should never be used to fill the municipal treasury. Start by enforcing blue zones and ensure sufficient parking at new construction with tailored solutions to prevent nuisance.

167017.explanation: 30 km/h speed limits inside built-up areas increase traffic safety, especially for cyclists and pedestrians. The speed must fit the road's design and function. This ensures safe, calm, and livable residential neighborhoods.

167018.explanation: Care must be accessible and of good quality for everyone. The beliefs of a care provider should never be decisive; professionalism and quality are paramount. The municipality should not make distinctions that unnecessarily complicate care.

167019.explanation: Price is a factor but should never be decisive. The municipality must find a good balance between affordability, quality, and continuity of care. This keeps care accessible and responsible for clients and municipal finances.

167020.explanation: The municipality must be close by and accessible to all residents. Offices in small communities lower thresholds, help less digitally skilled residents, and strengthen the feeling that the municipality is there for and by the residents. A pilot in small communities is preferred.

167022.explanation: Collaboration with other municipalities, for example in ICT, lowers costs, increases digital security, and makes specialized knowledge more available. This works more efficiently, reduces vulnerability, and keeps services to residents reliable and affordable.

167023.explanation: When hiring civil servants, quality, craftsmanship, and integrity must come first. Everyone deserves equal opportunities, but positions are filled based on suitability. This keeps the municipality professional, effective, and focused on good service.

167024.explanation: Transparency increases trust. With an online data portal, residents can easily see how municipal funds are spent. This strengthens oversight, involvement, and the awareness that the municipality manages tax money responsibly.

167025.explanation: Vacancy requires action, but extra money is not an end in itself. The focus is on better conditions: fewer rules, mixed land use, vacancy levies, and faster permits. Targeted efforts can help where needed, but always with attention to effectiveness and entrepreneurial initiative.

167026.explanation: Healthy municipal finances provide space for choices in the future. By managing debts carefully and repaying them faster where possible, we limit risks and interest expenses and protect residents from tax increases.

167027.explanation: Shelter policy requires careful, national decisions. The municipality must primarily focus on safety, livability, and facilities for residents. Extra efforts on shelter put pressure on villages and neighborhoods and do not fit the scale and capacity of De Bilt.

167028.explanation: Freedom of choice comes first. The municipality should not prescribe what anyone eats. Sustainable options are fine, but not as an obligation. Facilitate variety and let council members and visitors choose for themselves.

167029.explanation: Wind turbines do not fit the green and village character of De Bilt. In search area 67, they conflict with the interests of KNMI, UMC, and Defense and cause nuisance through shadow flicker, vibrations, and damage to landscape and nature.

167030.explanation: Biodiversity and animal welfare are important, but do not require a separate full-time civil servant. These tasks should be integrated within existing policy. This prevents extra bureaucracy and keeps the organization efficient and affordable.

167031.explanation: In Brandenburg West, the municipal executive left residents out in the cold regarding the decision on natural gas-free housing. Participation in initiatives must therefore always be voluntary. Residents decide if and when they participate, without obligation or coercion.

167032.explanation: In the housing crisis, we must not unnecessarily delay or make building more expensive. Rainwater harvesting is important but not a strict requirement for every project. Choose tailor-made solutions per location so that housing remains feasible and affordable.

167033.explanation: By designating the entire Jagtlust Estate as a municipal monument, we protect the manor house and the surrounding greenery. This prevents unwanted new construction, such as a new town hall behind the manor house, and preserves this heritage for future generations.

167034.explanation: Accessible library facilities are important, but reopening all BiebPoints is not self-evident. Assess each community based on need, accessibility, and costs. Focus on tailor-made solutions and smart alternatives instead of fixed locations everywhere.

167036.explanation: An arthouse cinema, like we have in Het Lichtruim, can add to the cultural offer but is not a core task of the municipality. Initiative and operation primarily belong to entrepreneurs and residents. The municipality can facilitate, but only if financially feasible and broadly supported.

167038.explanation: Good street lighting increases safety and the feeling of security. Streets must be well lit even after 10:00 PM, especially around residential areas, schools, sports clubs, and routes to stations. Safety weighs heavier here than limited energy savings.

167039.explanation: De Bilt is not a shelter location for extra intensive care. We do not want the relocation of Fivoor to our municipality nor a psychiatric asylum seekers center. These plans do not fit the scale of our villages and put safety and livability under pressure.

167040.explanation: Safety of women and girls is not a side issue. By expanding and accelerating the action plan, we address insecurity and intimidation more specifically. Prevention, enforcement, and quick follow-up increase the feeling of safety in public spaces.

167041.explanation: A visible police station increases accessibility, trust, and quick follow-up of reports. Investigating a return to De Bilt is wise to strengthen safety and improve cooperation between residents, police, and municipality.

167042.explanation: 750 homes in the Spoorzone is too massive and does not match De Bilt’s village character. Smaller-scale building prevents traffic and parking problems and ensures quality, greenery, and support. Livability, not quantity, must be leading.

167044.explanation: The former car scrapyard Akkie Stomphorst is a logical place for housing. With around 60 homes, a disturbed site is properly redeveloped. The municipality must demonstrate reliable governance and keep agreements made with the initiator and neighbors.

167045.explanation: Taking over debts does not solve the causes and is unfair to others. The municipality must support young people with guidance, debt restructuring, and prevention so that they gain control over their finances and progress sustainably.

167046.explanation: Paid parking does not fit the village character of Bilthoven and drives away visitors and entrepreneurs. Parking policy should not be a revenue model for the municipality. Provide sufficient free parking and enforcement where needed.

167047.explanation: Expansion of the blue zone in Oude Dorp helps reduce parking nuisance from long-term parkers. This keeps parking available for residents and visitors without introducing paid parking. Tailor-made solutions and enforcement ensure livability.

167048.explanation: Safety for cyclists and pedestrians is important, but traffic policy must not be one-sided. In De Bilt, the car is a necessity for many residents, not a luxury. Therefore, tailor-made solutions per road and neighborhood are needed, balancing all forms of transport.

167050.explanation: Poverty reduction is necessary to prevent debts, exclusion, and bigger problems. Spending less often leads to higher costs later. However, money must be used effectively, focusing on activation, customization, and helping people become self-reliant again.

167051.explanation: Communal living forms such as senior cohousing promote flow-through, independence, and mutual care. They reduce loneliness and relieve care. By supporting this, the municipality creates suitable homes for the elderly and space for families and starters.

167052.explanation: Meeting places strengthen cohesion and prevent loneliness. Every neighborhood should have space where residents can meet. This can be a community center, neighborhood center, or other informal place suited to the scale and needs of the area.

167054.explanation: Youth care is not a cost item but an investment in the future. Children should never be the victims of financial limits. The municipality must ensure that help remains available where needed, focusing on quality and continuity of care.

167055.explanation: Good mental health care for young people is essential, but free care for everyone up to 25 is not a municipal task and not affordable. The focus must be on quick access, quality, and prevention, in collaboration with health insurers and the national government.

167056.explanation: Parenting and media use are primarily the responsibility of parents and schools. The municipality should not continuously launch new campaigns but focus on its core tasks and provide support where necessary.

166994.explanation: A consultative referendum must be introduced because it gives citizens the opportunity to easily voice their opinion on (major) dossiers. Forza has been advocating this for years because citizens often seem sidelined in participation.

166996.explanation: Many residents appreciate Sunday openings, but we must continue to respect Sunday rest regarding loading and unloading for neighbors: they also want a day of rest. The position of small retailers must also be supported.

166998.explanation: Immigrants already here must integrate much better within the same budget. Mastery of Dutch is a basic skill and also requires personal effort. To keep resources manageable, an asylum stop must come quickly. No extra asylum center in our municipality!

167000.explanation: Forza has always opposed a fireworks ban. Now that it, unfortunately, seems to be coming nationwide, a central fireworks show can be useful, but we must consider our municipality’s expanse: maybe Maartensdijk should have its own show.

167001.explanation: Solar panels belong on roofs, not in nature: there they are even harmful!

167002.explanation: Sounds sympathetic, but this is a normal task of parents. The municipality already has huge debts. If parents cannot pay, the U-pass provides support.

167003.explanation: Forza wants to support the community life, both in sports, arts, and culture.

167004.explanation: Safety is a priority for Forza. Currently, there is far too little street supervision, especially in the evening and at night. Forza wants to free up our enforcement officers after office hours to supervise mainly unsafe and dark places.

167005.explanation: Safety is a priority for Forza, and (new) coffee shops do not belong in that.

167006.explanation: We must protect the unique nature around our villages from new construction. We have enough space within the villages, provided it is on a village scale, of course, so no apartment buildings. As far as Forza is concerned, there will be no building in the meadows around Maartensdijk and other vulnerable nature areas.

167008.explanation: 40% social rental housing is excessive and not a healthy mix of population groups. It creates new neighborhoods like Overvecht and the Bijlmer. The current policy of 30% is more than sufficient.

167009.explanation: High-rise buildings, such as those currently planned around the Spoorzone Bilthoven (7 floors), Schapenweide (8 floors), and Maartensdijk (5 floors), do not fit our village character. They are also unnecessary because we already have sufficient plans for the growth of our own residents.

167010.explanation: Housing speculation is undesirable, but this is not the solution: why shouldn't you be allowed to rent out your own home? Overpopulation is at the root of many (housing) problems: we need to tackle that.

167011.explanation: Given the current housing shortage, we must allow this, although naturally everyone deserves a 'normal' home. Overpopulation is at the root of many (housing) problems: we need to tackle that.

167013.explanation: In some exceptional cases this is understandable, but it should not become the standard, as it can easily lead to abuse. So: tailor-made solutions in very urgent cases, but always as an exception.

167014.explanation: It is legally forbidden for municipalities to engage in income politics (although they often do). A basic income is a communist principle, and Forza does not support that. Moreover, there are already many income support measures for low-income citizens.

167015.explanation: It has never been proven that this truly helps, for example with the climate. It takes parking spaces away from 'normal' cars, to the benefit of people who can easily afford an expensive electric car. They usually already have their own charging spot.

167016.explanation: Forza is strongly against paid parking, so also against parking permits. In neighborhoods with structural parking nuisance from commuters to/from, for example, Utrecht, a free permit for residents and their visitors can be considered.

167017.explanation: In residential areas 30 km/h is often excellent, but on our long, through roads it is a bad idea: there are usually separate cycle paths there.

167018.explanation: This promotes segregation and Forza opposes that.

167019.explanation: Currently, FAR too much is spent on healthcare in general, especially youth care. Municipalities, including ours, are nearly going bankrupt because of it. So yes: keep a close eye on costs and counter fraud, but quality is also important.

167020.explanation: Limited opening hours, for example for picking up a passport or driver's license, should also be possible in the villages. For a small fee, they can also be delivered to the workplace.

167022.explanation: Cooperation is obvious and can save costs. However, we must ensure sufficient (democratic) control and say.

167023.explanation: When hiring staff, the best candidate must be chosen: nepotism is unacceptable.

167024.explanation: Transparency is fundamental for a well-functioning democracy, so a great idea!

167025.explanation: Vacancy is bad for the atmosphere in a shopping center, and the municipality must do its best to combat vacancy. But direct funding goes too far: that should be done by the market, that is not what tax money is for.

167026.explanation: Forza is for healthy finances and we must stop nonsensical expensive projects, such as an unnecessary new town hall, while the current one can simply be renovated. But citizens must not suffer from higher taxes or fewer services because of this.

167027.explanation: Forza does not want any additional asylum seekers in our municipality, not even dispersed as some parties want. The nearly 200 current asylum seekers we are now hosting may of course stay.

167028.explanation: People must be able to make their own choices. No nannying!

167029.explanation: Wind turbines cause a lot of nuisance and are an eyesore. They do not belong in our nature.

167030.explanation: Animal welfare is very important to Forza, but hiring a full-time officer for it is excessive. It is largely a national responsibility.

167031.explanation: Freedom of choice for citizens is paramount: Forza will never force anyone off gas.

167032.explanation: Of course, but these measures have long been mandatory through the building regulations.

167033.explanation: Jagtlust is a characteristic symbol of our municipality: it must be protected, just like the surrounding park.

167034.explanation: This is important for our villages and can be done efficiently with, for example, a mobile library.

167036.explanation: A nice idea, but there are already cinemas within 10 minutes by bus, in Utrecht. Our municipality has no money for this, and experience shows there is too small an audience.

167038.explanation: Our municipality is very dark and feels unsafe in many places in the evening and at night, especially for (female) cyclists and pedestrians. The current left-wing council dimmed the street lighting after 10 p.m. to 70% and after midnight even to 50%, and Forza finds this unacceptable.

167039.explanation: Four years ago, Forza was the only party strongly opposed to the arrival of the TBS clinic Fivoor at Berg en Bosch. There are now talks about expansion, which Forza considers unsafe and unacceptable.

167040.explanation: The current action plan is weak: a hotline and some paper measures. It must be accelerated and expanded, with making street harassment punishable by law via the General Local Ordinance (APV) and keeping the street lighting fully on all evening and night. That really helps.

167041.explanation: The police station must return to De Bilt: reporting crimes in Baarn is far too cumbersome.

167042.explanation: Forza likes to build in the Spoorzone, but on a village scale. 750 homes is far too many; it creates a concrete, Overvecht-like neighborhood with 7-story flats, too much traffic and unsafe conditions. Forza supports the residents' plan for much fewer homes, preferably about 350.

167044.explanation: Forza wants to protect the green countryside, and therefore no building there. We build within the red contours, and there is sufficient space there: Spoorzone, Schapenweide and much more. But village-style, so not the planned 7- and 8-story flats.

167045.explanation: Low-income residents are already supported, but taking over debts sends the wrong message: people must take responsibility and be careful not to overspend.

167046.explanation: Paid parking is unacceptable for Forza: it does not fit our villages. Everyone must be able to run a quick errand or visit family or friends. Whether that is on foot, by bike, or sometimes by car. Stop the nannying!

167047.explanation: This is unnecessary: let's first enforce the current blue zone properly, so entrepreneurs get parking spots again (max. 2 hours) to receive their customers.

167048.explanation: Traffic safety is a spearhead for Forza, especially for cyclists and pedestrians; they are often children or elderly. But cars are also very important in our sprawling municipality with limited public transport, and Forza will not drive them away.

167050.explanation: Many millions are currently being spent, and municipal debts are rising sharply. It could be done a bit less.

167051.explanation: An old wish of Forza, although investments by the municipality must be very limited: the municipality should mainly stimulate and facilitate.

167052.explanation: Every village deserves its own place to come together and meet each other, although these should be efficient and modest.

167054.explanation: The municipality spends more and more on youth care every year, and this cannot continue, otherwise we will go bankrupt. Moreover, much is reimbursed that is unnecessary, and that waste needs to be eliminated. Good care, but only for those who really need it.

167055.explanation: A nice idea, but for a municipality like ours, with huge debts (128 million), completely unaffordable.

167056.explanation: Social media use is a big problem among young people, and the municipality certainly has a role here, alongside schools and parents. However, money is not the solution, but upbringing and awareness, at home and at school.

166994.explanation: We support clear participation. This includes allowing a referendum as an option to participate in decision-making.

166996.explanation: That is turning back the clock. In a 24-hour economy, it should be possible to do your shopping on any day of the week. That is up to the entrepreneur.

166998.explanation: Integration is of great importance to embrace newcomers. It starts with learning the language and then cooperating with our society. More opportunities must be utilized in work, sports and play, as well as education.

167000.explanation: If this helps against the unrest, it is worth trying. It should not be structural. We should see if it works, and otherwise stop this environmental pollution. Birds and other animals suffer greatly from it.

167001.explanation: Solar panels on agricultural land are an option that takes precedence over wind turbines. However, the power grid can no longer handle the solar energy.

167002.explanation: In a country with so much water and an influx of foreigners, school swimming is extremely important.

167003.explanation: Sports clubs that are struggling may be supported. However, the long-term viability must be considered.

167004.explanation: BOAs (enforcement officers) are relatively expensive positions. They are not always equipped for heavier tasks. Yet visibility and thus presence is very important. This is also what we hear from residents.

167006.explanation: This must be considered per situation. Smaller communities need more expansion to maintain livability. Then the red boundary should not be restrictive.

167008.explanation: This ratio is skewed and cannot be built profitably. 30% is an acceptable standard.

167009.explanation: This is an open question/statement. What is high? We already have buildings of 10 floors. The issue is fitting into the environment.

167010.explanation: No second homes or rental homes should be allowed.

167011.explanation: Partly yes, partly no. It is arbitrary. They were originally homes for vacation purposes.

167013.explanation: The goal should be to get out of social assistance and contribute to society again.

167014.explanation: There are already schemes set up by the national government. The municipality does not need to add anything on top. First, let's look at the effects in other municipalities in this area.

167015.explanation: First, we must inventory whether more charging points are really needed. This will differ per neighborhood. A generic wish is too broad and careless.

167016.explanation: Parking permits have ultimately become the new cash cow. Look at Utrecht. We support creating blue zones with a maximum parking time. Enforcement should be part of that. That is cumbersome and expensive.

167017.explanation: 30 km/h as a speed limit within the built-up area is a good ambition. Streets do not get wider, but cars do.

167018.explanation: If you need care, imposing requirements regarding religion or belief is not appropriate. We do not have the luxury of being able to choose.

167019.explanation: Quality is a much more important focus than price. Home care requires attention and dedication.

167020.explanation: We must look at what services can be usefully offered in the centers. A counter does not need to be open 5 days a week.

167022.explanation: In many cases, it is better if there can be cooperation on certain matters to achieve a higher technical level and share costs.

167023.explanation: Diversity should not be leading in hiring policies. It must be about the quality of the candidate.

167024.explanation: We do not think that opening data regarding expenditures leads to more insight. It will cause more unrest.

167025.explanation: For livability in shopping areas, it is important that there are no vacant spots. Allowing pop-up stores temporarily could be a solution.

167026.explanation: You pay interest on debts. That is not bad in itself, but it is money that cannot be used elsewhere. Debts are unavoidable because the national government is unwilling to pay enough for the services municipalities must provide. Still, it is good to aim for a healthy financial situation.

167027.explanation: Small groups are easier to integrate into a neighborhood or small community. Better social control can take place, which also helps the refugee.

167028.explanation: Meals are offered twice a year during long meetings. Dietary wishes can be communicated, but imposing them strictly is not something we want to worry about.

167029.explanation: Wind turbines should be at sea, because they achieve the highest yield there. In our municipality, which lies in a low-wind area, wind turbines are not useful. Moreover, at least five turbines must be placed at one location to build the infrastructure cost-effectively. Such locations are not available.

167030.explanation: It is important to have a stronger staffing for control of animal welfare policy and biodiversity monitoring. Whether that means one person full-time or two part-timers is a question we still want to explore. We are for stronger staffing.

167031.explanation: It is clear that a homeowner must be able to decide whether or not to disconnect from gas. The market is very uncertain, and therefore people should not be forced to go off gas.

167032.explanation: It will be a topic to discuss when talking with a project developer. What are the costs per home? Where will the water go? There is still much uncertainty. No new restrictions should be introduced that further frustrate building.

167033.explanation: Jagtlust should obtain protected status. It is valuable to our municipality as an estate. As an estate, it can be better protected against ambitious plans.

167034.explanation: Libraries can contribute to literacy and are therefore very important. They can also play a role in social cohesion.

167036.explanation: A film house is not a priority for this municipality. There are sufficient possibilities in surrounding municipalities.

167038.explanation: Where LED lighting is on, dimming after 10:00 PM is not very useful. To increase street safety, LED lamps should especially be prioritized in attention areas.

167039.explanation: Given the experiences with psychiatric and forensic care, we consider it undesirable to further increase this capacity.

167040.explanation: This is a point of great attention. Therefore, more urgency is needed in drafting a good plan and properly inventorying areas considered unsafe.

167041.explanation: For residents, having a physical, low-threshold police station is important, now that it has disappeared in recent years. Although we want the police to be more visible on the street. We wholeheartedly support an investigation.

167042.explanation: The 750 homes were a heavily negotiated compromise within the council. Fewer would be acceptable. Originally, the plan was for 500 homes with sufficient parking spaces. With such a number of homes (500) and all existing plans, we are still building what was intended.

167044.explanation: This project should have been built a long time ago, but was blocked based on vague and unreasonable demands. Many council members were very positive about this plan.

167045.explanation: The municipality should not get young people out of debt by taking over their debts.

167046.explanation: This is an undesirable idea. Moreover, it must be outsourced to a commercial operator. That operator will not only serve Bilthoven but will want more. A no-go!

167047.explanation: A blue zone can counter parking nuisance coming from Utrecht. However, it remains possible that a displacement effect will occur, causing other neighborhoods to suffer. Still, it is worth testing.

167048.explanation: You cannot put cyclists and pedestrians at the center. That tends to be anti-car harassment. We support free choice of transportation.

167050.explanation: Poverty alleviation has a purpose. As long as its effectiveness is not in question, there should be no cuts.

167051.explanation: This is not the municipality's task and it therefore should not invest money in it. The municipality also has no land to host such a project. However, the municipality can make its wishes known in discussions with developers.

167052.explanation: Although this sounds very appealing, these are expenses for the municipality, and there is no money for it. Priorities lie elsewhere, but we do acknowledge that certain neighborhoods may have a need for this.

167054.explanation: Youth care is increasingly a cost item. It is important that the money is spent effectively. We believe that resources must have and retain a relation to the number of care recipients. So, if fewer children need help, the budget should be adjusted, and vice versa.

167055.explanation: This is a blank check whose scope we cannot estimate. Care up to 18 years falls under the municipality, which receives compensation from the national government for this. This group then transfers to the health insurer.

167056.explanation: It is not the municipality's task to teach children through an awareness campaign how to deal with social media. For children, that responsibility primarily lies with parents and schools. For older people, it is family members. This is not a municipal task.

166994.explanation: Referenda are costly to organize and often lead to unfeasible outcomes and disappointment among voters. We believe elected representatives are chosen to make important decisions.

166996.explanation: Sunday closure is no longer appropriate. Entrepreneurs should be free to decide whether they want to open on Sundays.

166998.explanation: It is very important that asylum seekers integrate well into our society and can participate fully. This includes, among other things, learning Dutch and accepting the main Dutch norms, values, and customs. Investing firmly in this prevents problems in the future.

167000.explanation: A central fireworks show contributes to mutual connection and conviviality in our municipality and is a nice alternative to the ban on private fireworks use. We ensure that a central fireworks show involves all villages in our municipality!

167001.explanation: The power grid is currently completely full. Installing more sustainable energy generation in the coming years has little use. We would rather invest in grid reinforcement, energy storage (batteries), or geothermal energy.

167002.explanation: The current system of swimming lessons is sufficient in our view, although attention to long waiting lists is needed. Burdening schools with the organization of school swimming also takes time away from education.

167003.explanation: Exercising is healthy and fosters social connection. Moreover, sports prevent physical and mental health complaints. Therefore, it pays off to have a good offer of sports clubs. The starting point is that a financial contribution from the municipality should not be structural.

167004.explanation: We see an increase in loitering youth and troublemakers. This was painfully clear, for example, during New Year's Eve. Also, with the possible arrival of an asylum seekers' center (azc), it is good to invest in more enforcers.

167005.explanation: Coffeeshops add nothing to our municipality and rather cause nuisance.

167006.explanation: There is much need for affordable owner-occupied homes, and these can in some cases be well realized on the outskirts of a village. Moreover, these locations are often better accessible for traffic.

167008.explanation: Instead of social rent, we prefer affordable owner-occupied homes that you own yourself. With an owner-occupied home, housing costs remain the same, and maintenance and sustainability improvements are more rewarding. The social rental sector in the Netherlands also does not function well, partly due to skewed renting.

167009.explanation: Our municipality has a village character; housing construction must fit the character of the respective village and target group. For families, we prefer to build owner-occupied homes with a garden instead of flats. Apartments for elderly or starters are preferably no higher than five floors.

167010.explanation: (Affordable) owner-occupied homes are intended for (starting) home seekers and not for investors who rent them out. By implementing purchase protection, we keep homes more affordable and accessible for the intended target group.

167011.explanation: This depends on the specific case. Given the large housing shortage, it is understandable that holiday homes are sometimes permanently inhabited. At the same time, we want to prevent holiday parks from being permanently inhabited, for example, by migrant workers.

167013.explanation: We believe that social assistance benefits should not be a fixed source of income but only a temporary (emergency) solution. People should earn their own income and therefore apply for jobs. At the same time, we highly value informal caregivers and want to support them well where necessary.

167014.explanation: In our view, this is a task for the national government, not the municipality.

167015.explanation: Electric driving results in less CO2 emissions and should be facilitated and encouraged as much as possible. Moreover, with smart charging stations, we can relieve the power grid by using car batteries as temporary energy storage.

167016.explanation: Parking pressure on the street can be better regulated through permits. This is good for the livability and traffic safety of the neighborhood. In shopping areas, parking should of course remain free during opening hours. Any permit system should therefore apply there in the evening hours.

167017.explanation: Traffic to and from our villages must be able to flow smoothly and safely; therefore, on some arterial roads, the maximum speed limit should be 50 km/h. Outside arterial roads, the speed limit is already 30 km/h in many places and should remain so.

167018.explanation: This rule makes providing care unnecessarily complex and expensive. It is important that a care provider delivers good and professional care; factors such as religion or culture are not relevant in this.

167019.explanation: When selecting care providers, the right balance between quality and price is essential. If we only look at price, there is a risk that quality will be insufficient, which helps no one.

167020.explanation: The CDA supports a small and effective government. A municipal desk in every village center would simply be too expensive.

167022.explanation: By cooperating more with other municipalities, you can improve services while reducing costs. After all, you benefit from the experience civil servants have gained in other (larger) municipalities.

167023.explanation: We must choose the most suitable candidate available for a vacancy. Naturally, what someone brings to the team as a whole also plays a role. However, forced diversity targets do not contribute to higher quality civil servants.

167024.explanation: Clear insight into what a municipality does and what it costs leads to more involvement from our residents. It also allows the municipal government to account more clearly to the municipal council and citizens.

167025.explanation: Nothing is as uninviting as vacant retail and hospitality premises. Therefore, we want the municipality to sometimes give starting entrepreneurs an extra boost, for example, by providing free advice, helping with communication, or accelerated permitting.

167026.explanation: High debts also mean high interest charges, which is a waste of money. If the municipality has borrowed (too) much and the debt becomes (too) high, we must repay faster and spend less in other areas.

167027.explanation: Under the distribution law, we are obliged to accommodate asylum seekers in De Bilt as well. We find it just and fair to cooperate in this, but only if the accommodation is small-scale and spread throughout the municipality. Therefore, we are opposed to a large-scale asylum seekers' center at Berg & Bosch.

167028.explanation: We believe that municipal council members and citizens can perfectly well decide for themselves what they eat; the government does not need to intervene. It should instead focus on new housing, safe cycling paths, or better youth care.

167029.explanation: The CDA is a strong advocate for sustainable energy generation, but currently the power grid is very full, and when it is very windy, we often cannot properly utilize wind energy. We therefore want to first invest in grid reinforcement and energy storage before building more wind turbines.

167030.explanation: We prefer to spend money on more housing construction, short waiting lists in youth care, sustainable school buildings, safe cycling paths, or better sports facilities rather than on a civil servant concerned with the welfare of hedgehogs and bats.

167031.explanation: We believe homeowners should decide themselves how and when to make their homes more sustainable or gas-free. Moreover, we prefer a government that encourages and stimulates rather than one that forces or prohibits.

167032.explanation: Due to climate change, we increasingly face water nuisance. Therefore, it is important that we can properly collect and controlledly drain rainwater. Rainwater could also be used for flushing toilets (greywater system).

167033.explanation: We must be proud of and careful with our history, and therefore properly protect valuable and special monuments. Jagtlust is part of that. We also believe that Jagtlust should remain a home for our local democracy and administration.

167034.explanation: We are in favor of a small and efficient government. Opening Library Points in every village of our municipality is, in our view, too expensive. We do want to have at least one primary school with a library in every village so that children are always encouraged to read.

167036.explanation: We find the offerings for young people between approximately 15 and 18 years old in our municipality to be very limited. They are not yet allowed to go to pubs and therefore have few opportunities to meet and go out with friends. An arthouse cinema could be a good solution for this.

167038.explanation: In places where it improves (traffic) safety, it is worth considering, but very bright street lighting can also be annoying for residents. Therefore, what is wise may vary per neighborhood.

167039.explanation: Our municipality, as well as Den Dolder, already has a lot of forensic care and incidents occur here regularly, sometimes with far-reaching consequences. It is therefore logical that we do not expand this form of care further in our municipality.

167040.explanation: The safety of women and girls, as well as that of men and boys, deserves the highest possible protection. It is good that the municipality has an action plan. However, we believe the current action plan is sufficient and that its expansion adds little except more cost.

167041.explanation: A municipal investigation like this takes time and money, while the decision is not made by the municipality but by the police themselves. We can better spend that money on more municipal enforcement officers.

167042.explanation: The CDA is a great supporter of housing construction, including in the Spoorzone. But it must fit within the environment. Consider the road network, number of parking spaces, school capacity, etc. We think 750 homes is too many for this location and would prefer around 600 homes.

167044.explanation: The CDA considers this an excellent location for new homes, and there is already a fine plan from enthusiastic project developers to build affordable housing here. As far as we are concerned, let's get started as soon as possible!

167045.explanation: We believe residents are responsible for their own income and financial management. If young people face (financial) difficulties, the municipality offers a safety net and help but does not simply take over debts. We do provide help and guidance to get out of this situation.

167046.explanation: Good accessibility by car is very important for shopkeepers and entrepreneurs in Bilthoven because they have many customers from outside our municipality. Free parking is part of this. With the housing plans around the center, we want to ensure there are more free parking spaces.

167047.explanation: In our opinion, there is no reason to expand the blue parking zone. If there is, we first need to invest in more enforcement of blue parking zones, as currently it hardly happens. So a blue zone doesn’t make much sense now.

167048.explanation: Traffic policy should be a balanced mix of modes of transport. For many residents, the car is an indispensable means of transport, and public transport is also important in traffic policy. Therefore, it is about the mix, not primarily about cyclists or pedestrians.

167050.explanation: We want our residents to be able to take care of themselves and not be dependent on the government. That is why we prefer to help people find a job instead of giving them 'free money' as sometimes happens now.

167051.explanation: Communal living arrangements for the elderly are very good against loneliness and can also reduce social care costs because people can support each other more.

167052.explanation: A meeting place or community center contributes to cohesion and a pleasant, livable neighborhood. We see good examples of this in Westbroek and Hollandsche Rading. Such initiatives should also be encouraged in other neighborhoods. The starting point, however, is that they must be financially self-sufficient.

167054.explanation: Spending less on youth care is not a goal in itself. The goal is to have as few children in youth care as possible because our young people are resilient, healthy, and happy. If that succeeds, care costs will automatically decrease.

167055.explanation: Currently, youth mental health care automatically stops when young people turn 18. In many cases, these young people are still developing mentally. Due to the loss of free youth care, they fall into a care vacuum. Free mental health care up to 25 years reduces this risk.

167056.explanation: Increasing research shows that smartphones and social media have a very negative impact on our youth. It causes a lot of social pressure and stress. It is therefore good to educate parents and children on how to deal with this.

166994.explanation: According to the SGP, elections are the best referendum. By voting for a political party, voters give a direction for the coming period. Before decisions are made, society must be well heard, and instruments such as citizens' initiatives already exist.

166996.explanation: We live in a ‘hyper-nervous society’ where performance pressure, acceleration, and individualism have gone too far and seriously threaten the well-being of young and old (source: RVS). A moment of rest is not a luxury but absolutely necessary. The SGP wants to restore Sunday as a collective Christian day of rest.

166998.explanation: More money is not a solution for better integration of newcomers. There are various initiatives for integration of newcomers into society, such as the refugee support center. Responsibility also lies with the newcomers themselves.

167000.explanation: The SGP does not believe it is the municipality’s task to organize fireworks shows.

167001.explanation: We need farmland to produce our food. Providing for our own food supply is vital, especially in these uncertain times. Therefore, the municipality must preserve and cherish the farmland we still have for our farmers. No solar panels on food-growing fields.

167002.explanation: The initiative for school swimming should be left to the school itself and be a choice, not an obligation. If a school wants to teach pupils to swim, the municipality can make funds available, especially for families under the poverty line.

167003.explanation: When sports clubs have concrete plans to become financially healthy and sustainable, the municipality contributes financially. Recently, the SGP took the initiative to retain income from waste paper collection. The executive wanted to cancel this collection.

167004.explanation: Safety is a priority for the SGP. The SGP hears too much about youth nuisance. Enforcement officers have a role in tackling this nuisance. Recently, the SGP submitted a motion to allocate more money for safety. This motion was adopted.

167005.explanation: The SGP is against coffeeshops. Soft and hard drugs are harmful to public health and addictive. Therefore, the SGP does not support facilitating and thus normalizing these.

167006.explanation: The housing vision states: first build within the red contours. If that is not possible, then explore if building outside the red contours is feasible. The SGP wants to adhere to that policy and therefore cannot say 'agree' or 'disagree' here. Let's just get to work!

167008.explanation: The SGP supports more focus on affordable homeownership. The municipality, together with housing corporations and project developers, works on a suitable housing supply. The share of social rental, cheap ownership, and mid-range rental is tailored to local needs per village, not based on one standard for the entire municipality.

167009.explanation: According to the SGP, high apartment buildings do not fit the village character of our municipality.

167010.explanation: The SGP believes that people dependent on private rental housing also deserve a home. Therefore, the SGP disagrees with this statement. However, the SGP supports introducing purchase protection for affordable owner-occupied homes.

167011.explanation: A holiday home is meant for recreation. That is why it is called a holiday home. Nevertheless, there can be (distressing) situations where living long-term in a holiday home is legitimate. The municipality must first develop a policy for this and map out the consequences.

167013.explanation: Caregivers deserve support and appreciation. The SGP regards caregiving as no less important than paid work. An additional advantage is that by supporting caregivers, formal care is financially relieved.

167014.explanation: The SGP sees no reason for a system change. A basic income creates more dependency on the municipality. Moreover, the incentive to work is lower. This is undesirable given large shortages in the labor market. A basic income leads to higher taxes for people who work.

167015.explanation: Charging points for electric cars must be installed in Hollandsche Rading. Furthermore, the SGP supports a policy where parking facilities for electric cars do not come at the expense of parking spaces for other cars. So such charging spots should be in addition to existing parking places.

167016.explanation: Parking nuisance can be addressed with enforcement, not with parking permits. Parking permits bring administrative burdens and costs. Moreover, requiring a parking permit is not welcoming for visitors of neighborhood residents.

167017.explanation: What problem is this a solution for? If the road allows for a safe 50 km/h, the SGP prefers camera enforcement for offenders rather than converting the street to a 30 km/h zone. So it depends on tailoring the approach to each street type. Also, necessary speed bumps can cause nuisance.

167018.explanation: A care recipient feels most at home with a care provider or institution that shares their views. That builds trust. The SGP finds it logical that people needing care should be able to choose a care provider that suits them, of course as far as that care is available.

167019.explanation: According to the SGP, the focus must be on the quality of the home care organization. A cheap home care provider delivering mediocre work is ultimately expensive. Quality over price, therefore!

167020.explanation: For years, the SGP has advocated for the small communities, more precisely: green villages. With a municipal counter in our green villages, municipal services from the town hall will be better and closer to us as residents. This reduces the gap between residents and the town hall.
