167022.explanation: Cooperation is good, but the SGP does not see an immediate reason to increase it further. For every cooperation, the SGP considers it important that the municipality’s and its villages’ own identity remains intact.

167023.explanation: When hiring civil servants, the SGP believes the focus should not be on diversity, but on quality, professionalism, motivation, dedication, knowledge, and skills.

167024.explanation: Residents want to know what happens with their tax money. Therefore, the SGP also believes good governance is transparent about how tax money is spent. An online data portal makes expenditures clear.

167025.explanation: Shopping areas must remain lively and vital; vacancies do not help. Therefore, funds must certainly be allocated to combat vacancies in shopping areas. This benefits the business climate as well as consumers. Paid parking does not belong in efforts to prevent vacancies.

167026.explanation: Having debts can sometimes be a good choice, for example if needed to make a good investment that pays off. The SGP considers it important that debt stays as low as possible and is repaid as quickly as possible to keep municipal finances healthy.

167027.explanation: The SGP believes more conditions are needed for refugee reception. Think of support, safety, whether a reception center fits the village and community, and the group composition. Small scale is also important. Open communication and transparency are crucial from the start!

167028.explanation: Meat and fish are healthy. Fish contains important nutrients needed, such as fish oils. Meat is rich in vitamins and zinc (source: Nutrition Centre). Eating meat and fish provides income for farmers. The SGP believes serving meat and fish should be encouraged!

167029.explanation: The SGP does not want to sacrifice valuable municipal land for wind turbines. There is also too little space for this. Wind turbines cause much nuisance and are not a stable energy source. The SGP is in favor of small wind turbines at farms.

167030.explanation: The SGP considers biodiversity and animal welfare very important. More civil servants for this will not improve things. The SGP prefers stimulating initiatives and working groups on biodiversity. Animal welfare is already regulated by legal obligations and supervision.

167031.explanation: The SGP believes the choice to disconnect from gas must always be with the residents themselves. They must not be forced in any way by the government. Therefore, the SGP voted against plans to disconnect the Brandenburg West neighborhood in Bilthoven from gas.

167032.explanation: Capturing rainwater is a good goal but depends, according to the SGP, on the possibilities at the building site. If options exist, like creating a wadi or water tank, that is good. However, exceptions may exist where rainwater capture is not possible.

167033.explanation: Based on existing reports and expert advice, the SGP believes the entire Jagtlust estate must be designated a municipal monument. The Jagtlust estate and park belong together and must be preserved for the future.

167034.explanation: Reading is good for the development of young and old. This is supported by scientific research. In times of declining reading, the municipality should encourage that in every village or neighborhood a well-accessible library point is available within a reasonable distance.

167036.explanation: The SGP believes subsidizing a new cinema is not a task for the municipality. There are already enough cinemas. The money is better spent on keeping municipal taxes low, investing in school buildings, or maintaining public spaces.

167038.explanation: Street lighting CAN improve safety for residents but can also have the opposite effect. It can deter criminals but help escape routes. Moreover, people can be disturbed by lighting at night. The SGP wants decisions on lighting to be made per location.

167039.explanation: This is existing policy established in June 2023.

167040.explanation: The SGP stands for the safety of women and girls but believes attention must also be paid to the safety of other groups, such as caregivers.

167041.explanation: The police are indispensable in the villages and neighborhoods of the municipality of De Bilt. Visibility is important and preventive. Police presence informs them what is happening, and residents know how to reach the police. The SGP supports establishing a police support point in the green villages.

167042.explanation: The SGP finds the adopted plan too massive and voted against it. The massiveness does not fit the village character and causes, among other things, parking and traffic problems. Another plan will have more support and thus faster realization of these homes without lengthy procedures.

167044.explanation: The SGP supports housing development and awaits the plans for this location. The SGP will ensure the plan proposes affordable owner-occupied homes.

167045.explanation: The SGP favors preventing debts. Taking over debts is too easy. Debt counseling should be made accessible, in the SGP’s view, with the help of buddies and personal service. Think of achieving goals such as starting education or finding employment.

167046.explanation: Paid parking increases costs for residents and addresses a problem that does not exist. The SGP believes free parking stimulates the local economy because a shopping area then attracts more consumers to shop and stay.

167047.explanation: The SGP sees no reason to expand the blue parking zone in the Old Village. There is sufficient parking space. The municipality need not create solutions for problems that do not exist.

167048.explanation: It must concern a safe traffic flow for all road users together. We also have motorized traffic. Therefore, it makes no sense to focus solely on cyclists and pedestrians. When roads are worked on, vulnerable road users are taken into account.

167050.explanation: Unfortunately, we see more poverty around us. The SGP wants to encourage that for residents affected by poverty, besides financial aid from the municipality, help is also offered by societal organizations and churches.

167051.explanation: The SGP knows there is demand for such living arrangements. A knarrenhof is a beautiful form of community living, where people look out for each other. Concrete initiatives for knarrenhofs encounter difficulties in our municipality. The SGP wants to act decisively here.

167052.explanation: The SGP believes community spirit and meeting each other benefit society. This does not mean every neighborhood must have a community center or similar. The SGP lets the need from a neighborhood lead. Moreover, many churches and (sports) clubs provide opportunities for social interaction.

167054.explanation: The shortfall in youth care is mainly a problem because the national government does not provide sufficient funds. Vulnerable youth are central in youth care. The focus must be on guiding this group as well as possible, who must not become victims of financial shortfalls.

167055.explanation: It is important for young adults that mental health care remains accessible.

167056.explanation: There is a mental health crisis in the Netherlands, especially among young people. This is partly due to the negative impact of social media. Intensive use can be harmful to the mental health and development of children. It is high time to start a campaign on this!

166994.explanation: A referendum is always a yes or no vote. This removes all nuance. The municipal council is elected as a representation of all residents. Their job is to weigh all interests and viewpoints. A referendum does not do justice to good decision-making and open discussion.

166996.explanation: We consider an economy that centers on human well-being and where it is normal to rest essential. By rest, we also mean the possibility of Sunday rest for individuals and families. Upon reconsideration, we support Sunday shop closures.

166998.explanation: Integration of newcomers through participation is an important priority for us. Every resident must be able to fully participate in society. This strengthens mutual connection within society.

167000.explanation: We are positive about organizing or having a fireworks show, but in all communities. This can be in cooperation with the social organizations of each community.

167001.explanation: Locations for solar fields must always be carefully weighed. This includes considering whether they fit into the landscape, are safe for health, and are feasible with the existing electricity grid. The ChristenUnie is not opposed to solar fields beforehand.

167002.explanation: In the context of equal opportunities for all children, we find school swimming important to contribute to the development of children.

167003.explanation: Associations are responsible for their own financial position. The municipality can assist families who cannot afford the membership fees.

167004.explanation: In reducing crime and increasing the feeling of safety on the street, enforcement officers (BOAs) play an important role.

167005.explanation: Prevention and reduction of addiction contribute to strengthening the safety of our society. Having coffeeshops is not, in our view, the right way to prevent or reduce addiction.

167006.explanation: The ChristenUnie favors a balanced assessment of where building outside the red contours is permissible and where not.

167008.explanation: The ChristenUnie supports affordable housing. Not only social rental housing is a solution here, but also affordable owner-occupied and mid-range rental housing. For new construction, we apply 30% social rental and at least two-thirds of the homes affordable.

167009.explanation: Some ongoing and proposed housing projects involve forms of high-rise buildings. These projects cannot be halted. We are in favor of vigorous construction at these large locations.

167010.explanation: There is currently an owner-occupancy obligation of one year. We want to maintain this to ensure houses are inhabited and not just bought for profit. People must also have the opportunity to buy a home themselves and not only live there at an excessively high rent.

167011.explanation: Holiday homes are not intended to be permanent residences. Before permanent residence is made possible, infrastructure and the municipal tax system must first be adjusted accordingly.

167013.explanation: We believe in a society where people care for each other. We find that doing volunteer work, including informal care, should be made easier for people while maintaining their benefits.

167014.explanation: We do not believe it is a municipal task to experiment with this. It is a responsibility that lies at the national level.

167015.explanation: Promoting electric driving by providing fixed parking spaces and ensuring sufficient charging points, in our opinion, contributes to more sustainable car use.

167016.explanation: The Netherlands faces a large and still growing vehicle fleet. A consequence of this is the difficulty in finding parking spaces. Residents and visitors will have to find their own way.

167017.explanation: The maximum speed for all roads must be carefully considered. Lowering the speed throughout the entire built-up area is undesirable and sometimes even impossible. For example, there are main roads for which 50 km/h is appropriate, even in the built-up area. Also, for example, buses must be able to continue driving.

167018.explanation: Care tailored to who you are is a goal we must always strive for. However, we must also admit that with the shortages in care, this is not always possible. Where possible, we commit ourselves to this.

167019.explanation: Quality of care delivery is more important than price. Still, price is also important. Therefore, for the ChristenUnie, the focus is on the best possible price-quality ratio in care delivery.

167020.explanation: For municipal services that are well accessible to all residents, we find it important that there are municipal counters in the smaller communities.

167022.explanation: The ChristenUnie believes that services should remain close to the resident. If the municipality is insufficiently organized for specific municipal tasks, we favor intensive cooperation with other municipalities.

167023.explanation: This is not a political matter but a concern for the civil service organization. Our civil service organization is currently very vulnerable. We can imagine that recruitment focuses primarily on knowledge, expertise, and service to our municipality.

167024.explanation: The current financial documents, such as the spring memorandum, budget, autumn memorandum, and annual accounts, are already available on the municipal website. The Bilt can also be compared with other municipalities online. We do not see the necessity of an online data portal.

167025.explanation: Society is constantly changing, including our shopping streets. For each building, the best use should be determined. However, it is not the municipality’s task to subsidize running a store. The municipality can provide good regulations and an appropriate area vision.

167026.explanation: Municipal debts are often the result of investments made in the community. Having debts is part of this. We accept a generally accepted debt repayment pattern. However, restraint in further investments is necessary for a healthy financial future.

167027.explanation: Our strong preference is for locations for small-scale housing for asylum seekers spread over the communities in De Bilt. We believe this best promotes the integration of asylum seekers into our society. It also ensures a dispersed and thus broader support base in our villages.

167028.explanation: This is not a political issue for us. It is a personal choice whether or not to eat food with or without meat or fish. Everyone has their own responsibility in this.

167029.explanation: When making location choices for wind turbines, the impact on human health, natural values, and the electricity grid must be carefully considered. However, we are not inherently against wind turbines. We do see that finding suitable locations in our municipality is a challenge.

167030.explanation: It is the responsibility of the civil service organization to determine what type and scale of function and tasks are necessary to strengthen biodiversity and animal welfare.

167031.explanation: The national government requires us to be gas-free by 2050. It is up to the municipality to find the best solution for each neighborhood to make this happen. Participation in this joint solution remains everyone’s own choice, or they can provide their own heating.

167032.explanation: For us, it is important that the municipality commits to building sustainable homes and making existing homes more sustainable. This also includes rainwater harvesting.

167033.explanation: The ChristenUnie supports initiatives to make this estate a monument. Historical places must be preserved. However, this does not mean that nothing will ever change on the estate. But any change must always take into account the historical character of the estate.

167034.explanation: This depends on the possibilities that the library organization Idea sees to resume the BiebPunten (library points). Idea can also possibly enable further rollout of electronic book lending. The ChristenUnie wants to support reading.

167036.explanation: De Bilt has the location Het Lichtruim available for very diverse cultural activities. In our view, it can also be used as an art house cinema.

167038.explanation: We favor extra lighting at locations prone to insecurity. This can be on a flexible, temporary basis if necessary. We do not support having street lighting on at full strength everywhere in our municipality by default.

167039.explanation: The desirability of such an expansion depends on the exact nature of the care, the location, and its effects and risks on and for the environment.

167040.explanation: We pursue active combat against street intimidation and relationship violence such as femicide, (sexual) transgressive behavior, stalking, and domestic violence. An action plan is needed to implement these measures.

167041.explanation: We support strengthening public order and safety in De Bilt. Having a police station can enhance the visible presence of district officers and enforcement officers (BOAs).

167042.explanation: The housing shortage is large, but new homes must always fit within the existing environment. In our opinion, 750 homes do not fit. The number of homes must be maximized but remain livable so that living there is pleasant.

167044.explanation: We support housing construction as far as it fits within the conditions of the Hollandse Waterlinie and Natura 2000 area. When a plan is developed, it must be carefully weighed. Saying yes in advance to 60 new homes is careless. The ChristenUnie is not fundamentally opposed.

167045.explanation: Financial worries greatly contribute to instability in families. Preventing high debts among young people is essential for their future. Equally important is strengthening resilience against various forms of crime and addiction so that debts can be prevented.

167046.explanation: Paid parking negatively affects the commercial attractiveness of this shopping area.

167047.explanation: The Netherlands faces a large and still growing vehicle fleet. A consequence of this is the difficulty in finding parking spaces. Residents and visitors will have to find their own way.

167048.explanation: The Christian Union believes that the position of cyclists and pedestrians should play a more prominent role in traffic policy. This should also take into account the position of public transport and car traffic.

167050.explanation: Of course not! Supporting people with the lowest incomes is important to give them a full place in our society as well.

167051.explanation: We are strong advocates for providing space for housing and residential construction for specific target groups. This also includes concepts like ‘knarrenhof’ and other communal living forms where LIVING TOGETHER is central.

167052.explanation: The municipality must provide safe meeting places where residents can meet each other and engage in activities. This contributes to mutual involvement in the local living environment and looking out for one another.

167054.explanation: Youth care must remain well accessible and affordable for those who need it. Investing in young people is investing in the future.

167055.explanation: We find good accessibility and affordability of specific care for young people important. This way, we want to give everyone the best possible start in their further lives.

167056.explanation: Misuse of social media can lead to undesirable issues such as insecurity among children, relational violence, and even crime and radicalization.

166994.explanation: The corrective referendum has been adopted nationally by the SP in the House of Representatives; SP De Bilt considers a referendum a good tool to consult residents on important issues that deeply impact our municipality and to give them a voice.

166996.explanation: Disagree! Shopkeepers have the free choice themselves to open or close on Sundays. Let’s mainly support our local shopkeepers and not hinder them if they want to be open on Sundays. However, you could discuss with shopkeepers when they receive deliveries; on Hessenweg this is, among other things, a nuisance.

166998.explanation: SP De Bilt believes the municipality should invest more in integrating newcomers. With money for language education, work, culture, and neighborhood activities, newcomers can fully participate, contribute to society, and actively join the social life of De Bilt.

167000.explanation: Agree! Other municipalities already lead the way. A show together brings life and festive joy. The national ban on consumer fireworks is now in place; this is a great alternative. It maintains our tradition and is safe, connecting, and enjoyable this way.

167001.explanation: The SP supports sustainable energy, but solar panels must not harm agricultural land and food production. Agrivoltaic systems that combine energy and farming, and provide extra income for farmers, do receive support. Farmers and residents must be involved and have a say in this.

167002.explanation: Agree! Swimming is an important basic skill for children. Nowadays, parents have to arrange it themselves, and if you lack the financial means, your child might not learn to swim. In a country like the Netherlands, where there is much water, it is good if we work with local schools to make this possible again.

167003.explanation: Agree! Sports are important for our residents; they connect people, are good for health, social contacts, and strengthen our communities. The SP supports helping clubs that are financially struggling. However, we assume sports clubs demonstrate good stewardship.

167004.explanation: Agree! Safety in our neighborhoods must not be up for debate! More enforcement officers (boa's) means quicker and more effective action, responding to the demands and reports from residents. More capacity means more coverage and increased safety feelings in neighborhoods.

167005.explanation: We have exactly one coffeeshop in the municipality of De Bilt, where things run quietly and safely. No other coffeeshops have expressed interest in establishing themselves in De Bilt. Let’s focus more on housing our residents!

167006.explanation: SP De Bilt opposes building outside the red contour. We choose affordable infill within villages, close to facilities. Building in green areas damages landscape and nature and usually results in expensive homes. The open countryside must be preserved for residents and future generations.

167008.explanation: Agree! Housing is a right, but in De Bilt the need is high: social rent is scarce, waiting times are long, and young people leave. SP De Bilt wants faster building and at least 40% social housing, better flow, and affordable rent and purchase so residents can stay in their own village.

167009.explanation: Disagree! We have few building plots and sometimes very small ones; we build what fits the area. Besides ground-bound houses, we also build apartments. Due to the housing shortage, choices must be made; with only ground-bound homes, houses remain scarce and expensive while the demand is large.

167010.explanation: Houses are bought up and sold again at insane prices. This drives the housing market to extremes, making it too expensive, resulting in many people being unable to find suitable and affordable housing. Stop speculation! This keeps the housing market affordable in De Bilt.

167011.explanation: We have too few affordable homes in this municipality! In Hollandsche Rading, De Egelshoek, and Floris V, many people have lived together peacefully for years. If we don’t act, these people risk becoming homeless. Having a roof over your head is not a luxury but a right.

167013.explanation: Agree! Caregivers are crucial and indispensable and are sometimes greatly undervalued! Care has been cut for years, and it has been under pressure. We must support caregivers, not pressure them. Forcing them to apply for jobs makes it impossible to provide and continue their care.

167014.explanation: Agree! A pilot with a basic income for people on minimum income fits SP De Bilt because it provides security and peace of mind. It gives people confidence, reduces stress and bureaucracy, and makes it easier to care, learn, or find work. This way, we strengthen basic security for our residents.

167015.explanation: Disagree! More charging points take away scarce public parking spaces from many residents and benefit a small group of motorists. The costs are for everyone, but the benefits are not. Moreover, many residential areas and shopping centers already have parking spaces with electric charging points.

167016.explanation: Disagree! Due to the housing shortage, children stay at home longer and often depend on a car for work or study. Hardworking people with a company van are also affected. It leads to a waterbed effect and places an unfair financial burden on families where a car is a necessity.

167017.explanation: Disagree! The SP wants a safe neighborhood, but not everywhere 30 km/h. On ring roads and main streets, 50 km/h is fine as long as pedestrians and cyclists have safe, separate paths; otherwise, 30 km/h should be the norm. Smart measures and strict enforcement in the right places work much better than a universal limit.

167018.explanation: The SP believes that good care must be equal and accessible to everyone. Respect for background is fundamental but should not lead to separate care systems. We choose one strong public care system focused on the human dimension for everyone, regardless of faith or culture.

167019.explanation: Disagree! Home care is about people, not about being cheap. Selecting only by price lowers quality, causes staff shortages, and increases stress. The SP wants safe, personal care with fixed, well-paid staff so residents get the care they need.

167020.explanation: Agree! The SP strongly supports this because services must be close to people, especially for those less digitally skilled. Municipal offices in small communities ensure accessibility, personal contact, and equal treatment. The government must come to people, not the other way around.

167022.explanation: SP De Bilt supports cooperation with other municipalities, for example in ICT, if it improves service and saves costs. At the same time, local customization and resident involvement remain important so systems work smarter without losing Bilt’s interests.

167023.explanation: Disagree. Quality comes first, and precisely for that reason, diversity is essential. A diverse organization sees more, understands the neighborhood better, and delivers higher quality. We want a government that fairly reflects society so everyone has equal opportunities.

167024.explanation: Agree! The SP believes residents should be able to see how the municipality spends its money. An online data portal is fine, but accessibility for everyone is important. Residents should not only be able to view information but also be involved in policy and decision-making.

167025.explanation: The SP finds it necessary to tackle vacancy rigorously. Shops are important for jobs, livability, and social contacts in the neighborhood. By making more funds available, the municipality can invest in lively shopping areas where people like to come and shop locally.

167026.explanation: The SP believes the municipality must manage debt responsibly, but money should not come at the expense of care, education, or social services. Investing in residents is more important than paying off debts as quickly as possible. Stability and social policy go hand in hand.

167027.explanation: The SP supports small-scale and dispersed reception of refugees. This way people get decent and safe housing, can better integrate, and support in villages and neighborhoods remains. Decisions must be transparent and made in consultation with residents.

167028.explanation: The SP supports that municipal council meetings always offer vegetarian and vegan options. This promotes sustainable and inclusive eating but leaves choices for those who want meat or fish. The municipality thus sets an example without excluding people.

167029.explanation: SP De Bilt agrees with this statement. New onshore wind turbines lack support and harm the living environment. We choose offshore wind and solar panels on roofs. Abolishing the net metering scheme makes sustainability less attractive, while no one wants a wind turbine in their immediate surroundings.

167030.explanation: The SP supports efforts on biodiversity and animal welfare and believes this requires structural policy. A full-time official can ensure consistent, expert policy to protect nature and animals well and involve residents. Caring for nature and animals is not a side issue but a priority.

167031.explanation: SP De Bilt believes that residents must be able to fully choose whether to participate in projects to phase out natural gas. Participation must be entirely voluntary, with accurate information and guidance, so sustainability remains achievable without pressure or financial disadvantages for residents.

167032.explanation: The SP supports that new construction projects only proceed if measures for rainwater collection are in place. This way, neighborhoods are built sustainably and future-proof, floods are prevented, and it remains affordable and feasible for residents and developers.

167033.explanation: SP De Bilt has doubts. Heritage deserves protection, but giving monument status to the entire Landgoed Jagtlust estate could unnecessarily block future decisions. We want to carefully weigh what is needed for preservation, use, and support among residents, rather than imposing an all-encompassing designation.

167034.explanation: The SP wants to keep all BiebPoints and library branches in the municipality open in all neighborhoods. This way, culture, knowledge, and digital skills remain accessible to everyone, and social contacts and equal opportunities are strengthened for young and old across all neighborhoods.

167036.explanation: Disagree. The SP does not want to cut culture budgets, but we must also be realistic with public money. Instead of building a new cinema, we prefer to leverage the strength of the existing Lichtruim. This keeps culture affordable and nearby without unnecessary extra investments.

167038.explanation: Disagree. Running everything at full power is not necessary. Let’s focus on unsafe spots and busy cycling routes. By providing tailored measures in risk zones, we ensure safety where it really matters, instead of just turning all the lights fully back on everywhere.

167039.explanation: Agree! SP De Bilt is cautious about expanding psychiatric and forensic care. We were already critical when Fivoor came to Berg en Bosch. The site is already under pressure and residents experience nuisance. SP De Bilt does not want an expansion of heavy care cases, such as sex offenders.

167040.explanation: The SP, initiator of the safety action plan for women and girls, wants this plan to be accelerated and expanded. Every woman must feel safe. We demand better protection, reporting points, prevention, and increased awareness among residents and partners. Safety in De Bilt is a priority!

167041.explanation: SP De Bilt believes the municipality should investigate whether a police station can be re-established in De Bilt. This would allow residents to easily walk in, have personal contact with the police, and strengthen accessibility and safety in the municipality for everyone.

167042.explanation: SP De Bilt believes the Spoorzone should not be scaled down from the planned 750 homes. In fact, we would have preferred even 1,000 homes. The project offers unique opportunities for Bilt residents looking for housing, reduces the housing shortage, and provides affordable rental and owner-occupied homes.

167044.explanation: SP De Bilt supports building 60 homes on the former car scrapyard site on Utrechtseweg. With attention to affordable housing, market turnover, priority for local residents, sustainable construction, and nature, so that starters and families in De Bilt get a chance to find a home.

167045.explanation: The SP wants the municipality to take over young people’s debts and teach them to handle money responsibly. This gives youth a fair start without long-term restrictions. At the same time, this reduces pressure on debt counseling and the care system. Investing in the future works!

167046.explanation: Disagree! SP De Bilt opposes paid parking in the center of Bilthoven. We are a village: residents and visitors must be able to park freely; this affects both shopkeepers and residents. Parking problems should be solved locally and practically, not with expensive charges to residents.

167047.explanation: SP De Bilt agrees that expanding the blue zone should ensure commuters do not park their cars in the streets and that residents can still park in their own street. This keeps parking fairly distributed and clear, and preserves the village atmosphere.

167048.explanation: SP De Bilt believes cyclists and pedestrians should be central to the municipality’s traffic policy. Cars remain indispensable, but safety, accessibility, and good routes for children, elderly, and vulnerable residents have absolute priority so everyone can travel safely.

167050.explanation: Disagree! Spending less on poverty reduction increases stress and affects residents. With fast debt relief, debt forgiveness, reimbursements for sports, culture, and school supplies, the SP ensures residents make ends meet and have security, children can participate, and financial pressure decreases.

167051.explanation: The SP supports knarrenhofjes (senior courtyards) and communal living. This promotes social contact, independence, and wellbeing. It reduces loneliness and keeps housing affordable. This way, elderly people can live safely and with dignity in their own familiar neighborhood. A strong community is priceless for De Bilt!

167052.explanation: The SP wants a community center or meeting place in every neighborhood. This allows residents to meet, strengthen social ties, organize activities, and participate in shaping the neighborhood. Community centers foster cohesion, participation, and a livable, social environment for everyone.

167054.explanation: The SP opposes cuts to youth care. Children and youth must always have access to the care they need. Good youth care prevents problems later, supports families, and strengthens neighborhood livability. Investing in youth care is investing in the future.

167055.explanation: Agree! SP De Bilt wants free and accessible mental health care for young people up to 25 years old. The municipality must invest in local, easily accessible care with sufficient capacity so problems are addressed early and young people receive the support they need.

167056.explanation: Agree! In recent years, social media have become indispensable for youth-for identity, social contacts, the latest news, and sharing photos and videos. This is not without risks: what about privacy, risks of sharing images, and who can actually see this? Awareness is prevention.

166994.explanation: A referendum seems a clear democratic tool for major choices. But according to Volt, complex problems cannot be captured in just yes or no. Therefore, Volt advocates other forms of democracy, such as a citizens’ council and structural involvement of residents in decision-making.

166996.explanation: Volt wants to give individual entrepreneurs the freedom to decide whether they want to open on Sundays. Sunday offerings provide benefits to our residents and should not be prohibited.

166998.explanation: The municipality has an important role in integration, provided it is spent correctly. Investing in language, education, work, and encounters helps newcomers participate faster and prevents problems later. Good integration is an investment in coexistence, safety, and opportunities for everyone.

167000.explanation: A central fireworks show ensures a festive and safer New Year's Eve with less nuisance. Volt also sees room for cooperation with fireworks associations so that tradition, safety, and support come together.

167001.explanation: Solar energy is indispensable for the energy transition. At the same time, using agricultural land requires careful consideration per location so that food production, nature, and landscape are not pressured. Tailored solutions and clear frameworks are therefore necessary.

167002.explanation: Swimming skills are essential in a country full of water. Swimming lessons are often expensive, time-consuming, and have long waiting lists. School swimming increases opportunities for every child. Schools may choose this; the municipality must actively facilitate it.

167003.explanation: Sports clubs are important for health and social cohesion. Volt invests in sports and supports accessible and inclusive clubs. Contributions go to viable clubs with broad representation and healthy governance within clear frameworks.

167004.explanation: Volt sees safety as a shared responsibility. Enforcement officers (BOA’s) can contribute through visibility, prevention, and contact with residents, but not as a replacement for the police. Always in conjunction with investments in youth, care, public space, and social cohesion.

167005.explanation: Volt supports a realistic and regulated drugs policy. Blocking new coffee shops in advance does not work and can actually increase nuisance. The municipality must manage good distribution, clear rules, and safety, considering livability and health.

167006.explanation: New construction outside village borders puts the green and village character under pressure. Volt therefore prefers building within existing boundaries first, through infill and redevelopment. Only if truly necessary, and with nature preservation, can limited expansion be considered.

167008.explanation: Affordable housing is a top priority. We choose at least 30% social rental in new construction, supplemented with mid-rent and affordable owner-occupied homes. This ensures a balanced housing mix and livable, socially mixed neighborhoods. A higher percentage deters developers from investing.

167009.explanation: Low-rise and mid-rise buildings fit well in the municipality’s streetscape, but Volt does not want to exclude all forms of high-rise buildings by definition. It must be assessed whether it is appropriate and whether it best meets the housing needs.

167010.explanation: A home is not an investment object but meant to live in. By stimulating owner-occupation, we keep homes available for residents and starters and combat speculation, within the possibilities offered by law.

167011.explanation: Volt chooses sustainable and structural housing solutions in suitable locations. Holiday homes and recreational areas are meant for recreation and nature and are not a good solution for permanent residence. This is also legally complicated. The municipality of De Bilt follows national policy on this.

167013.explanation: Informal caregiving is valuable and takes much time and energy. Volt believes that people with social assistance benefits who provide intensive informal care should not be obliged to apply for jobs. The municipality assesses this per case, within the Participation Act. Caring for each other should be recognized, not penalized.

167014.explanation: Volt supports a basic income: a fixed, unconditional income that provides social security. It stimulates development and care for each other and can reduce poverty and stress. A pilot in De Bilt can show what works, locally and nationally.

167015.explanation: Volt promotes electric driving. Due to growing demand, extra charging points are needed, which the municipality should designate. Residents can propose locations, after which the municipality finds a suitable spot. Charging at home is preferred to prevent grid congestion.

167016.explanation: Volt wants livability to be the priority in public space development. Parking nuisance does not contribute to this. By introducing a parking permit, fewer cars park, leaving more space for cyclists and pedestrians.

167017.explanation: 30 km/h as the norm within built-up areas makes streets safer and more livable. It reduces accidents, noise, and stress, and gives more space to cyclists and pedestrians. Redesign encourages safe driving behavior and time loss is minimal. On main roads, tailored measures remain possible.

167018.explanation: Always offering this choice comes at the expense of other factors such as expertise, availability, and distance. Volt is open to considering this but there are limits and care quality must always take priority.

167019.explanation: Volt believes selecting based on quality and continuity is more important than selecting based on price. In the long term, this is cheaper.

167020.explanation: Volt supports having a single fixed point of contact at the municipal counter, preferably at the same location as Library Points. Residents and businesses receive one contact person who follows up on questions, escalates internally, and ensures timely and clear answers. This makes the municipality more personal and manageable.

167022.explanation: Volt is a European party that believes in cooperation across borders. Locally, Volt actively seeks collaboration with neighboring municipalities. By sharing expertise and combining roles, such as one official serving multiple municipalities, we save costs and enhance quality.

167023.explanation: Everyone deserves equal opportunities, regardless of gender, background, religion, or whom you love. This does not happen automatically. Volt wants fair and inclusive workplaces. Therefore, diversity is a fixed part of hiring policies alongside suitability, leading to better understanding, policies, and representation.

167024.explanation: Transparency about municipal expenditures increases residents' trust and involvement. An accessible online data portal makes clear how community money is spent-only on large-scale topics where the municipality has direct discretion.

167025.explanation: Within its capabilities, the municipality must facilitate that owners, neighbors, and initiators have opportunities to counteract vacancy. Think of pop-up shops, anti-squatting, temporary squares or parks. Social value and a pleasant environment should be central in this.

167026.explanation: Volt wants to keep municipal finances healthy and work together with residents on a long-term financial plan. Core tasks such as poverty reduction, care, youth, and public spaces remain protected. The principle is: the user pays, with protection for the most vulnerable.

167027.explanation: Small-scale and dispersed shelter better suits our municipality. It creates more support and better integration. The Central Agency for the Reception of Asylum Seekers (COA) funds the shelter. The municipality retains control over location, integration, and neighborhood involvement through clear agreements.

167028.explanation: Volt wants the municipality of De Bilt to make the use of vegetarian and organic products standard, with sustainable local products where possible. This supports these markets and guarantees demand for sustainable products. The council has a role model function.

167029.explanation: The energy transition requires sustainable energy, such as wind energy. New wind turbines must be carefully integrated with attention to landscape, nature, and support. Excluding them outright does not fit a future-proof energy supply. Volt also sees opportunities for local energy cooperatives.

167030.explanation: Biodiversity and animal welfare are important to Volt. The municipality must have sufficient attention and expertise for this. Whether through a dedicated full-time position, a shared function with other municipalities, or another arrangement depends on what works best for De Bilt.

167031.explanation: Volt supports the transition away from gas but does not want to force residents who cannot afford it. The electricity grid is already overloaded, so switching is not always possible. Therefore, Volt advocates feasible steps with room for individual choices within joint projects.

167032.explanation: Climate change causes heavy rainfall to occur more often. Therefore, rainwater capture should be standard in new construction projects. This prevents water nuisance and makes the living environment future- and climate-resilient.

167033.explanation: Estate Jagtlust has great cultural-historical and landscape value and deserves protection. At the same time, designating it as a municipal monument is a serious instrument. Each building and component must be carefully weighed to determine the appropriate and effective form of protection.

167034.explanation: Library Points are important for meeting, information, and assistance, especially for less digitally skilled residents. By opening them in all villages, services come closer to home and reduce the digital divide. Preferably combined with a municipal counter.

167036.explanation: Volt sees that there are already multiple places within the municipality where films are shown. In Bilthoven, Het Lichtruim and De Woudkapel are proven locations, and Utrecht offers a wide range of arthouse films. Culture connects people, but Volt sees other initiatives where funds can be better used.

167038.explanation: Volt sees little added value in a standard 100% lighting everywhere and at all times. At each location, a balance can be made whether this increases safety, taking into account neighbors, sustainability, and nature. Sensors can possibly provide an intermediate solution.

167039.explanation: The demand for psychiatric and forensic care is high, as shown by long mental health care waiting lists. Volt believes everyone has the right to good and accessible care. Preventing expansion worsens problems and deprives people of necessary help.

167040.explanation: The safety of women and girls requires urgent and structural action. The plan is ready, but implementation must be faster and more effective. More prevention, better design of public spaces, and clear follow-up of reports are needed.

167041.explanation: Police response times are currently too long because there is no longer a police station in the municipality. Volt wants to investigate whether a central police station or branch is feasible. This can reduce the distance with the neighborhood officer, increase the feeling of safety, and improve arrest chances.

167042.explanation: The Spoorzone is suitable for higher-density housing due to its location near the station. This addresses the housing shortage while protecting green spaces and villages. Quality, livability, and participation are preconditions. Breaking open the plan causes unnecessary delays.

167044.explanation: Redeveloping outdated business parks into housing is a sustainable choice. This creates living space within existing areas and protects green and rural areas, provided plans fit well with the surroundings.

167045.explanation: Debt takeover is not debt forgiveness. This reduces stress and prevents higher debts, but only as part of a broader approach. Think of early detection, simplification of debt counseling, and aftercare. The takeover must lead to a sustainable improvement of the situation.

167046.explanation: Paid parking is not an end in itself but a means to reduce parking pressure and car traffic, for example on Julianalaan. This creates more space for cyclists, pedestrians, and a pleasant Bilthoven center. Volt wants to keep the parking garage under the De Kwinkelier shopping center free of charge.

167047.explanation: The blue zone is intended to discourage long-term parking by commuters and protect livability in the Old Village. Expansion can help, provided it is combined with good park-and-ride facilities. Without alternatives, the problem is only shifted.

167048.explanation: Volt wants cyclists and pedestrians to become the norm in the municipality. By making shopping streets and cut-through traffic routes bicycle streets where cars are guests, they gain a more central role in traffic policy.

167050.explanation: Poverty causes much stress and often leads to higher healthcare costs. By tackling poverty, people's well-being improves and healthcare demand decreases. This is good for residents and saves costs: poverty reduction works doubly.

167051.explanation: Communal living arrangements, such as 'knarrenhofjes,' help elderly people live independently and reduce loneliness and care pressure. Volt wants the municipality to support such initiatives so elderly can live comfortably and connected in their own neighborhood.

167052.explanation: Meeting places are important for social cohesion and well-being. The municipality can stimulate this by collaborating with local entrepreneurs, associations, and neighborhood initiatives. In combination with Library Points, meetings are organized accessibly and fittingly in the neighborhood.

167054.explanation: Volt sees that youth mental health care is under pressure. Too many young people wait too long for urgently needed help. By bringing more national mental healthcare funding to municipalities, there is room to expand care and preventive services so that youth receive quicker and better support.

167055.explanation: Three-quarters of mental health problems arise before the age of 25. During this phase, young people take important steps such as studying and building relationships. Mental health issues then have extra impact. Good and timely access to care and prevention is essential for this group.

167056.explanation: Raising awareness about social media falls under prevention and youth care and is therefore a municipal task. Budget is desirable because prevention later prevents problems and healthcare costs and better supports parents and children.

166994.explanation: Fracti3 considers resident involvement essential but does not see a referendum as the right instrument for complex decisions. We believe in good participation at the start and transparent decision-making. Residents must have influence and rely on careful choices by the council.

166996.explanation: Fracti3 believes entrepreneurs should decide when to open their shops. A general ban does not fit with trust and entrepreneurship. Sunday openings can contribute to vibrancy, residents' choice, and sustaining amenities.

166998.explanation: Fracti3 considers good integration essential for cohesion and safety. Investing in language, education, and participation prevents larger problems later. Integration is not optional: support comes with clear expectations about contributing to society.

167000.explanation: Fracti3 believes community funds must be used purposefully. A municipal fireworks show is costly and does not solve nuisance, insecurity, and damage during New Year's Eve. We prefer measures that truly contribute to a safe and livable celebration.

167001.explanation: Fracti3 wants to accelerate the energy transition. Solar panels on roofs, business parks, and infrastructure are preferred. Solar panels on agricultural land can be additional if carefully integrated and preferably combined with nature or agricultural use.

167002.explanation: Fracti3 believes swimming skills are a basic ability. School swimming increases safety, saves lives, and ensures equal opportunities for children, regardless of home situation. This is a meaningful investment.

167003.explanation: Fracti3 sees sports clubs as an essential part of livability and social cohesion in our municipality. If clubs face problems due to external developments, it is logical for the municipality to intervene timely and collaborate on future-proof solutions.

167004.explanation: Fracti3 believes safety requires visible presence and clear boundaries. In places where nuisance and insecurity arise, enforcement officers must be able to act quickly and be approachable. Additional special enforcement officers contribute to prevention, better signaling, and the feeling of safety in neighborhoods and villages.

167005.explanation: Fracti3 opposes the normalization of drug use. New coffee shops can put extra pressure on residential areas, cause more nuisance, and attract crime. In a municipality with relatively small centers, restraint is therefore appropriate.

167006.explanation: Fracti3 believes that housing construction should primarily take place within the village boundaries. By building within the red contour, we protect the landscape, strengthen the villages, and ensure that homes are better connected to amenities and public transport. Deviations are only allowed when there is clear added value.

167008.explanation: Fracti3 believes that housing construction must align with the needs of different communities and locations. Fixed percentages can actually hinder movement. We opt for customization: building what is necessary to promote mobility and livability and to keep the housing market balanced.

167009.explanation: Fracti3 wants to address the housing shortage and does not fundamentally rule out higher buildings. In well-accessible locations, such as near public transport hubs, shops, and other amenities, apartments can actually contribute to affordable housing for starters, students, and seniors. Construction must fit the environment.

167010.explanation: Fracti3 believes that homes are meant for living in, not for speculation. A self-occupancy obligation increases opportunities for starters and movers and prevents price inflation caused by investment.

167011.explanation: Fracti3 believes that permanent residence in holiday homes is not a general solution to the housing shortage. Holiday parks are often not designed for this, which can lead to unsafe situations. However, urgent cases deserve tailored solutions, where the municipality carefully weighs what is appropriate on a case-by-case basis.

167013.explanation: Fracti3 values informal care and recognizes that situations differ per person. Therefore, the municipality must provide customized solutions. Those providing intensive informal care are given allowances and do not have to apply for jobs; those who can combine work or reintegration with informal care do so based on realistic agreements.

167014.explanation: Fracti3 believes that basic security is the foundation for social participation. A pilot with a basic income can show whether more trust and fewer rules help people move forward. Such a pilot must be carefully designed and evaluated so that policy is based on what truly works.

167015.explanation: Fracti3 believes that cleaner driving should be practically facilitated. More charging points make electric driving accessible for residents without driveways. This must take into account limited parking space and livability in neighborhoods.

167016.explanation: Fracti3 believes that parking in residential neighborhoods should, in principle, remain free. Parking problems require targeted solutions, such as better enforcement or smarter use of space, not permit systems that burden residents additionally.

167017.explanation: Fracti3 considers traffic safety important, but a general 30 km/h limit does not suit all roads. In residential areas and near schools, 30 km/h is logical; on access roads it is not. We choose customization.

167018.explanation: Fracti3 believes that quality and professionalism must lead in care. Choosing based on religion or culture does not fit a secular government. Selection based on identity undermines equal treatment and may exclude people such as women, LGBTQ+ individuals, or minorities.

167019.explanation: Fracti3 holds that good home care revolves around quality and continuity. Excessive focus on the lowest price leads to high work pressure and lower quality care. Quality must weigh more than price alone.

167020.explanation: Fracti3 believes the municipality must be accessible to everyone. Not everyone is equally mobile or digitally skilled. Local counters make service more personal and prevent residents from getting stuck on (digital) barriers.

167022.explanation: Fracti3 finds cooperation with other municipalities logical where it delivers quality and efficiency, for example in ICT, cybersecurity, and digital services. This pools knowledge and capacity while preserving local authority.

167023.explanation: Fracti3 views diversity as not secondary, but as contributing to better decision-making, inclusive services, and understanding of diverse residents. Quality always comes first: people are hired based on suitability, not identity. Diversity and quality reinforce each other.

167024.explanation: Fracti3 believes residents must be able to see how public money is spent. Transparency increases trust and democratic control. An accessible data portal makes municipal choices clear without complicated documents.

167025.explanation: Fracti3 finds vibrant shopping areas important for livability. Vacancy leads to decline and undermines amenities. The municipality must actively counter this by promoting reuse, cooperating with entrepreneurs, and, if necessary, investing temporarily and targeted.

167026.explanation: Fracti3 believes municipal finances must be healthy but not at the expense of necessary investments. Funds should also be used for housing, care, and livability. Responsible financial policy requires balance, not paying off debt at any cost.

167027.explanation: Fracti3 believes refugees must be accommodated humanely as quickly as possible. Small-scale reception sounds appealing but is unrealistic due to the housing shortage and lack of suitable locations. The municipality must choose a feasible solution that does not add extra pressure on housing seekers.

167028.explanation: Fracti3 wants to promote sustainability but not through mandatory symbolic measures. What people eat is a personal choice. The municipality can and should make sustainable options attractive but imposing a standard does not fit an open and inclusive governance culture.

167029.explanation: Fracti3 believes the municipality should not exclude sustainable energy generation out of hand. Whether new wind turbines are suitable depends on location, impact on the environment, and support among residents. The municipality must carefully weigh each situation.

167030.explanation: Fracti3 considers biodiversity and animal welfare important but sees this as a responsibility that should be integrated across several policy areas. Instead of a separate full-time position, Fracti3 chooses an integrated approach where existing teams consistently pay attention to this.

167031.explanation: Fracti3 finds resident participation important, but the energy transition sometimes requires collective solutions. If everyone can always opt out individually, necessary steps won’t be taken. Therefore, we choose support, good information, and clear choices, together with residents.

167032.explanation: Fracti3 believes new construction must be future-proof. Rainwater collection prevents flooding and damage. For new projects, it is logical to arrange this well immediately as a standard part of responsible building.

167033.explanation: Fracti3 holds that Landgoed Jagtlust has great cultural-historical and landscape value. Protecting the entire estate preserves this place for future generations. This does not mean locking everything down, but handling plans and developments carefully.

167034.explanation: Fracti3 believes library facilities are essential for accessibility, language development, and social participation. BiebPoints are low-threshold places where you can go for reading, digital skills, and information close to home. Reopening in all communities strengthens equal opportunities.

167036.explanation: Fracti3 sees culture as a bonding agent. A cinema is a meeting place that contributes to vibrancy and cultural participation. Investing reinforces the cultural offer in Bilthoven.

167038.explanation: Fracti3 believes residents, especially women, must feel safe in the evening. Street lighting must be sufficient and evenly distributed to provide good visibility and overview. Dimmed lighting can increase insecurity. Smart lighting prevents glare and enhances safety.

167039.explanation: Fracti3 believes people needing care must receive appropriate support. At the same time, expanding forensic and psychiatric care requires careful consideration of safety, support, and livability. The municipality must assess what is responsible and suitable on a case-by-case basis.

167040.explanation: Fracti3 considers the safety of women and girls, both in public space and behind the front door, an absolute priority. Insecurity requires quick and visible action. By expanding and accelerating the action plan, the municipality can better address risks and strengthen prevention.

167041.explanation: Fracti3 finds visible and nearby police important for safety and trust. By investigating whether a police station in De Bilt is possible, it can be examined how accessibility and cooperation can be improved. The investigation does not oblige but provides insight.

167042.explanation: Fracti3 considers the Spoorzone suitable for many homes because of its location near the station and shops. Here, starter, student, and senior homes can be built without extra pressure on the landscape. The plan for 750 homes has already been approved.

167044.explanation: Fracti3 is positive about redeveloping outdated, environmentally burdensome sites. The former car scrapyard is not open landscape. If sustainable housing is built here with attention to biodiversity, the municipality should provide space through a careful decision-making process.

167045.explanation: Fracti3 believes young people with debts should be well supported but does not view assuming debts as the right solution. The municipality must focus on guidance, debt counseling, and providing perspective so young people can become independent again.

167046.explanation: Fracti3 believes the center of Bilthoven must remain well accessible. Paid parking is a barrier for visitors and pressures entrepreneurs. Free and logical parking belongs to an accessible village center.

167047.explanation: Fracti3 finds parking pressure in residential areas must be addressed. Due to proximity to public transport and exit roads, commuters sometimes park here for long periods. Targeted expansion of the blue zone can protect residents, with regular evaluation.

167048.explanation: Fracti3 believes traffic must be safe and sensible. By putting cyclists and pedestrians at the center, safety increases for vulnerable road users. This does not mean banning cars but putting safety first.

167050.explanation: Fracti3 considers poverty reduction a core task. Less investment increases problems and leads to higher costs later. We choose targeted support that increases basic security and prevents people from getting stuck.

167051.explanation: Fracti3 believes seniors should be able to age comfortably in their own village. Communal living arrangements reduce loneliness and enable longer independent living. Investing in this prevents heavier care later.

167052.explanation: Fracti3 sees social interaction as essential for livable neighborhoods. A community center strengthens social cohesion, prevents loneliness, and makes support accessible. This is a basic facility.

167054.explanation: Fracti3 sees that youth care needs to be different and smarter, but less funding often means later and more intensive help. We want to invest specifically in prevention and low-threshold support to prevent heavy care.

167055.explanation: Fracti3 believes that young people must receive help quickly. Municipalities cannot make mental health care free after the age of 18; that is a national responsibility. What we do: focus on low-threshold support and cooperation with care providers, strengthen prevention, and push for better, affordable mental health care.

167056.explanation: Fracti3 believes children must grow up safely in a digital world. We support parents and children with knowledge and tools that increase awareness about the risks of social media and smartphone use. Our starting point is: better later than too early, for example only from age 14.

statements.title: The BiebPunten (library points) in Groenekan, Hollandsche Rading, Westbroek, Bilthoven North, Bilthoven South and De Bilt must be reopened.

titleParts.glossary: A place where you can pick up and drop off library books.

titleParts.text: in Groenekan, Hollandsche Rading, Westbroek, Bilthoven Noord, Bilthoven Zuid en De Bilt moeten weer open.

statements.moreInfo: The BiebPunten in Groenekan, Hollandsche Rading, Westbroek, Bilthoven Noord, Bilthoven Zuid and De Bilt were closed as of July 1, 2025. Residents could, among other things, reserve books there and pick up reserved books.

statements.title: The residents of the municipality of De Bilt must be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van gemeente De Bilt moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The new development project in the Spoorzone must be smaller in scale than the 750 planned homes.

titleParts.text: Het nieuwbouwproject in de Spoorzone moet

titleParts.glossary: Places where only a few people are housed together. Not many people in one place.

statements.moreInfo: In the Spoorzone at Bilthoven station a new-build project is planned. In 2025 the municipal council of De Bilt decided that a maximum of 750 new homes may be built there.

statements.title: The municipality must allow solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality must ban shops from being open on Sundays.
No tall residential apartment blocks may be built in the municipality anymore.
The municipality should spend more money on integrating newcomers into society.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld uitgeven aan

titleParts.glossary: Newcomers learn the language and customs of the Netherlands.
People who are new to the Netherlands.

statements.title: The municipality must prevent the establishment of new coffeeshops.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality must prevent new construction outside the existing village boundaries (red contour).
statements.moreInfo: In the municipality of De Bilt building is only allowed within the "red contour." The province sets this as the boundary of the built-up area, which is why we also call it the provincial red line. Only in very exceptional cases is building allowed outside it.

statements.title: The municipality must introduce a parking permit in neighborhoods with parking problems.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in wijken met parkeeroverlast een

titleParts.glossary: A permit to park in a certain place. You have to pay for that right.

statements.title: The municipality must repay its debts as quickly as possible.
The municipality must conduct a trial of a basic income.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet een proef doen met

titleParts.glossary: With a basic income, people receive a fixed amount per month from the government. They do not have to apply for jobs or perform socially useful work in return for the money.

statements.title: The municipality must allocate more funds to combat vacancy in shopping districts.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld uittrekken om

titleParts.glossary: When buildings or homes are not used and are therefore vacant.

statements.title: The municipality must help young people with high debts by taking over their debts.
The municipality must oppose the construction of new wind turbines.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de bouw van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: These are modern wind turbines that convert wind power into electricity and can be up to 150 meters high.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality must support dispersed, small-scale shelter for refugees.
The municipality must restore street lighting to full brightness after 10:00 PM.
statements.moreInfo: The municipality of De Bilt dims street lighting after 22:00 to 70% and from midnight to 50%.

statements.title: The speed limit within built-up areas must be set to 30 km/h everywhere.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality must designate the entire Jagtlust estate as a municipal monument.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet van heel Landgoed Jagtlust een

titleParts.glossary: An old and notable building or object that is protected by the municipality.

statements.moreInfo: The country house Jagtlust (attached to the town hall) is a municipal monument, but the surrounding estate is not yet. The municipality of De Bilt can choose to grant the entire Jagtlust estate that monumental status. Spatial and building plans on the estate would then have to take into account extra rules and protections.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the expansion of psychiatric and forensic care institutions in De Bilt.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de uitbreiding van instellingen voor

titleParts.glossary: Psychiatric care is the treatment of people with serious mental problems. Forensic care is treatment ordered by a judge as part of a sentence or measure. Help for patients with an addiction or an intellectual disability may be included.

statements.theme: Officer for biodiversity and animal welfare
statements.title: The municipality must appoint a full-time official for biodiversity and animal welfare.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet een voltijds ambtenaar aanstellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Many different kinds of plants, animals and fungi live on Earth. Species depend on each other to keep nature healthy.
That animals have a good and healthy life.

statements.title: The municipality must spend less money on poverty reduction.
At least 40% of the new homes to be built in the municipality must be social rental housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in de gemeente moet minimaal 40% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Cyclists and pedestrians must be central to the municipality's traffic policy.
The municipality must spend the same or less money on youth care.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet evenveel of minder geld uitgeven aan

titleParts.glossary: Support for young people with problems, for example mental health care, foster care, day programmes, and help with dyslexia.

statements.title: The municipality must allocate funds for an awareness campaign for parents and children about managing social media use.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld uittrekken voor een

titleParts.glossary: A series of advertisements to inform people about a topic and encourage them to think or change.

titleParts.text: voor ouders en kinderen over omgaan met social media.

statements.title: The municipality must invest money in establishing an arthouse cinema in Bilthoven.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should investigate whether a police station can be re-established in De Bilt.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should permit the construction of 60 new homes on the site of the former car scrapyard on Utrechtseweg in De Bilt.
De Bilt should reopen municipal service desks in the small villages.
The municipality should deploy more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety.
The municipality should introduce paid parking in the center of Bilthoven.
Residents should always be able to decide for themselves whether to take part in collective initiatives to transition off gas.

shootoutStatements.theme: Exemption from the job application obligation
shootoutStatements.title: People receiving welfare benefits who provide informal care to a family member or acquaintance do not have to apply for jobs.
The municipality should cooperate more with other municipalities, for example in the area of ICT.
The municipality should expand and accelerate the existing action plan for the safety of women and girls.
The municipality should open a community center, neighborhood center or other meeting place for residents in every neighborhood.
The municipality should expand the blue parking zone in the Oude Dorp in De Bilt.
Residents should be able to see via an online data portal what the municipality spends money on.
If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.
Anyone who buys a home in the municipality must live in it themselves.
Diversity should be a secondary consideration when hiring civil servants.
The municipality should commit to providing free mental health care (GGZ) for young people up to 25 years old.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.
The municipality should invest in knarrenhofjes and other communal living arrangements for older people.
At meetings of the De Bilt municipal council, food without meat or fish should be served by default.
The municipality should only permit new construction projects if measures for rainwater collection have been taken.
When selecting organizations for home care, the municipality should focus primarily on price.
